You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED(\"TODO(P1): Add implementation.\"); }\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `WritePageGuard`. Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer.h\n//\n// Identification: src/include/buffer/arc_replacer.h\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#pragma once\n\n#include <list>\n#include <memory>\n#include <mutex>  // NOLINT\n#include <optional>\n#include <unordered_map>\n\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\nenum class AccessType { Unknown = 0, Lookup, Scan, Index };\n\nenum class ArcStatus { MRU, MFU, MRU_GHOST, MFU_GHOST };\n\n// TODO(student): You can modify or remove this struct as you like.\nstruct FrameStatus {\n  page_id_t page_id_;\n  frame_id_t frame_id_;\n  bool evictable_;\n  ArcStatus arc_status_;\n  FrameStatus(page_id_t pid, frame_id_t fid, bool ev, ArcStatus st)\n      : page_id_(pid), frame_id_(fid), evictable_(ev), arc_status_(st) {}\n};\n\n/**\n * ArcReplacer implements the ARC replacement policy.\n */\nclass ArcReplacer {\n public:\n  explicit ArcReplacer(size_t num_frames);\n\n  DISALLOW_COPY_AND_MOVE(ArcReplacer);\n\n  /**\n   * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n   *\n   * @brief Destroys the LRUReplacer.\n   */\n  ~ArcReplacer() = default;\n\n  auto Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t>;\n  void RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type = AccessType::Unknown);\n  void SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable);\n  void Remove(frame_id_t frame_id);\n  auto Size() -> size_t;\n\n private:\n  // TODO(student): implement me! You can replace or remove these member variables as you like.\n  std::list<frame_id_t> mru_;\n  std::list<frame_id_t> mfu_;\n  std::list<page_id_t> mru_ghost_;\n  std::list<page_id_t> mfu_ghost_;\n\n  /* record entries in mru_ and mfu_\n   * this uses frame_id_t to guarantee no duplicate records for the same\n   * frame when they are alive */\n  std::unordered_map<frame_id_t, std::shared_ptr<FrameStatus>> alive_map_;\n  /* record entries in mru_ghost_ and mfu_ghost_\n   * this uses page_id_t but not frame_id_t because page_id is the unique\n   * identifier in ghost lists */\n  std::unordered_map<page_id_t, std::shared_ptr<FrameStatus>> ghost_map_;\n\n  /* alive, evictable entries count */\n  [[maybe_unused]] size_t curr_size_{0};\n  /* p as in original paper */\n  [[maybe_unused]] size_t mru_target_size_{0};\n  /* c as in original paper */\n  [[maybe_unused]] size_t replacer_size_;\n  std::mutex latch_;\n\n  // TODO(student): You can add member variables / functions as you like.\n};\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/include/buffer/arc_replacer.h",
    "numLines": 91,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 91

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/include/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.h"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// disk_scheduler.h\n//\n// Identification: src/include/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.h\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#pragma once\n\n#include <future>  // NOLINT\n#include <optional>\n#include <thread>  // NOLINT\n#include <vector>\n\n#include \"common/channel.h\"\n#include \"storage/disk/disk_manager.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief Represents a Write or Read request for the DiskManager to execute.\n */\nstruct DiskRequest {\n  /** Flag indicating whether the request is a write or a read. */\n  bool is_write_;\n\n  /**\n   *  Pointer to the start of the memory location where a page is either:\n   *   1. being read into from disk (on a read).\n   *   2. being written out to disk (on a write).\n   */\n  char *data_;\n\n  /** ID of the page being read from / written to disk. */\n  page_id_t page_id_;\n\n  /** Callback used to signal to the request issuer when the request has been completed. */\n  std::promise<bool> callback_;\n};\n\n/**\n * @brief The DiskScheduler schedules disk read and write operations.\n *\n * A request is scheduled by calling DiskScheduler::Schedule() with an appropriate DiskRequest object. The scheduler\n * maintains a background worker thread that processes the scheduled requests using the disk manager. The background\n * thread is created in the DiskScheduler constructor and joined in its destructor.\n */\nclass DiskScheduler {\n public:\n  explicit DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager);\n  ~DiskScheduler();\n\n  void Schedule(std::vector<DiskRequest> &requests);\n\n  void StartWorkerThread();\n\n  using DiskSchedulerPromise = std::promise<bool>;\n\n  /**\n   * @brief Create a Promise object. If you want to implement your own version of promise, you can change this function\n   * so that our test cases can use your promise implementation.\n   *\n   * @return std::promise<bool>\n   */\n  auto CreatePromise() -> DiskSchedulerPromise { return {}; };\n\n  /**\n   * @brief Deallocates a page on disk.\n   *\n   * Note: You should look at the documentation for `DeletePage` in `BufferPoolManager` before using this method.\n   *\n   * @param page_id The page ID of the page to deallocate from disk.\n   */\n  void DeallocatePage(page_id_t page_id) { disk_manager_->DeletePage(page_id); }\n\n private:\n  /** Pointer to the disk manager. */\n  DiskManager *disk_manager_ __attribute__((__unused__));\n  /** A shared queue to concurrently schedule and process requests. When the DiskScheduler's destructor is called,\n   * `std::nullopt` is put into the queue to signal to the background thread to stop execution. */\n  Channel<std::optional<DiskRequest>> request_queue_;\n  /** The background thread responsible for issuing scheduled requests to the disk manager. */\n  std::optional<std::thread> background_thread_;\n};\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/include/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.h",
    "numLines": 91,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 91
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/buffer_pool_manager_test.cpp"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// buffer_pool_manager_test.cpp\n//\n// Identification: test/buffer/buffer_pool_manager_test.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include <cstdio>\n#include <filesystem>\n\n#include \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\"\n#include \"gtest/gtest.h\"\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\nstatic std::filesystem::path db_fname(\"test.bustub\");\n\n// The number of frames we give to the buffer pool.\nconst size_t FRAMES = 10;\n\nvoid CopyString(char *dest, const std::string &src) {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(src.length() + 1 <= BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE, \"CopyString src too long\");\n  snprintf(dest, BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE, \"%s\", src.c_str());\n}\n\nTEST(BufferPoolManagerTest, DISABLED_VeryBasicTest) {\n  // A very basic test.\n\n  auto disk_manager = std::make_shared<DiskManager>(db_fname);\n  auto bpm = std::make_shared<BufferPoolManager>(FRAMES, disk_manager.get());\n\n  const page_id_t pid = bpm->NewPage();\n  const std::string str = \"Hello, world!\";\n\n  // Check `WritePageGuard` basic functionality.\n  {\n    auto guard = bpm->WritePage(pid);\n    CopyString(guard.GetDataMut(), str);\n    EXPECT_STREQ(guard.GetData(), str.c_str());\n  }\n\n  // Check `ReadPageGuard` basic functionality.\n  {\n    const auto guard = bpm->ReadPage(pid);\n    EXPECT_STREQ(guard.GetData(), str.c_str());\n  }\n\n  // Check `ReadPageGuard` basic functionality (again).\n  {\n    const auto guard = bpm->ReadPage(pid);\n    EXPECT_STREQ(guard.GetData(), str.c_str());\n  }\n\n  ASSERT_TRUE(bpm->DeletePage(pid));\n}\n\nTEST(BufferPoolManagerTest, DISABLED_PagePinEasyTest) {\n  auto disk_manager = std::make_shared<DiskManager>(db_fname);\n  auto bpm = std::make_shared<BufferPoolManager>(2, disk_manager.get());\n\n  const page_id_t pageid0 = bpm->NewPage();\n  const page_id_t pageid1 = bpm->NewPage();\n\n  const std::string str0 = \"page0\";\n  const std::string str1 = \"page1\";\n  const std::string str0updated = \"page0updated\";\n  const std::string str1updated = \"page1updated\";\n\n  {\n    auto page0_write_opt = bpm->CheckedWritePage(pageid0);\n    ASSERT_TRUE(page0_write_opt.has_value());\n    auto page0_write = std::move(page0_write_opt.value());  // NOLINT\n    CopyString(page0_write.GetDataMut(), str0);\n\n    auto page1_write_opt = bpm->CheckedWritePage(pageid1);\n    ASSERT_TRUE(page1_write_opt.has_value());\n    auto page1_write = std::move(page1_write_opt.value());  // NOLINT\n    CopyString(page1_write.GetDataMut(), str1);\n\n    ASSERT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid0));\n    ASSERT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid1));\n\n    const auto temp_page_id1 = bpm->NewPage();\n    const auto temp_page1_opt = bpm->CheckedReadPage(temp_page_id1);\n    ASSERT_FALSE(temp_page1_opt.has_value());\n\n    const auto temp_page_id2 = bpm->NewPage();\n    const auto temp_page2_opt = bpm->CheckedWritePage(temp_page_id2);\n    ASSERT_FALSE(temp_page2_opt.has_value());\n\n    ASSERT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid0));\n    page0_write.Drop();\n    ASSERT_EQ(0, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid0));\n\n    ASSERT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid1));\n    page1_write.Drop();\n    ASSERT_EQ(0, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid1));\n  }\n\n  {\n    const auto temp_page_id1 = bpm->NewPage();\n    const auto temp_page1_opt = bpm->CheckedReadPage(temp_page_id1);\n    ASSERT_TRUE(temp_page1_opt.has_value());\n\n    const auto temp_page_id2 = bpm->NewPage();\n    const auto temp_page2_opt = bpm->CheckedWritePage(temp_page_id2);\n    ASSERT_TRUE(temp_page2_opt.has_value());\n\n    ASSERT_FALSE(bpm->GetPinCount(pageid0).has_value());\n    ASSERT_FALSE(bpm->GetPinCount(pageid1).has_value());\n  }\n\n  {\n    auto page0_write_opt = bpm->CheckedWritePage(pageid0);\n    ASSERT_TRUE(page0_write_opt.has_value());\n    auto page0_write = std::move(page0_write_opt.value());  // NOLINT\n    EXPECT_STREQ(page0_write.GetData(), str0.c_str());\n    CopyString(page0_write.GetDataMut(), str0updated);\n\n    auto page1_write_opt = bpm->CheckedWritePage(pageid1);\n    ASSERT_TRUE(page1_write_opt.has_value());\n    auto page1_write = std::move(page1_write_opt.value());  // NOLINT\n    EXPECT_STREQ(page1_write.GetData(), str1.c_str());\n    CopyString(page1_write.GetDataMut(), str1updated);\n\n    ASSERT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid0));\n    ASSERT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid1));\n  }\n\n  ASSERT_EQ(0, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid0));\n  ASSERT_EQ(0, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid1));\n\n  {\n    auto page0_read_opt = bpm->CheckedReadPage(pageid0);\n    ASSERT_TRUE(page0_read_opt.has_value());\n    const auto page0_read = std::move(page0_read_opt.value());  // NOLINT\n    EXPECT_STREQ(page0_read.GetData(), str0updated.c_str());\n\n    auto page1_read_opt = bpm->CheckedReadPage(pageid1);\n    ASSERT_TRUE(page1_read_opt.has_value());\n    const auto page1_read = std::move(page1_read_opt.value());  // NOLINT\n    EXPECT_STREQ(page1_read.GetData(), str1updated.c_str());\n\n    ASSERT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid0));\n    ASSERT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid1));\n  }\n\n  ASSERT_EQ(0, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid0));\n  ASSERT_EQ(0, bpm->GetPinCount(pageid1));\n\n  remove(db_fname);\n  remove(disk_manager->GetLogFileName());\n}\n\nTEST(BufferPoolManagerTest, DISABLED_PagePinMediumTest) {\n  auto disk_manager = std::make_shared<DiskManager>(db_fname);\n  auto bpm = std::make_shared<BufferPoolManager>(FRAMES, disk_manager.get());\n\n  // Scenario: The buffer pool is empty. We should be able to create a new page.\n  const auto pid0 = bpm->NewPage();\n  auto page0 = bpm->WritePage(pid0);\n\n  // Scenario: Once we have a page, we should be able to read and write content.\n  const std::string hello = \"Hello\";\n  CopyString(page0.GetDataMut(), hello);\n  EXPECT_STREQ(page0.GetData(), hello.c_str());\n\n  page0.Drop();\n\n  // Create a vector of unique pointers to page guards, which prevents the guards from getting destructed.\n  std::vector<WritePageGuard> pages;\n\n  // Scenario: We should be able to create new pages until we fill up the buffer pool.\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < FRAMES; i++) {\n    const auto pid = bpm->NewPage();\n    auto page = bpm->WritePage(pid);\n    pages.push_back(std::move(page));\n  }\n\n  // Scenario: All of the pin counts should be 1.\n  for (const auto &page : pages) {\n    const auto pid = page.GetPageId();\n    EXPECT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pid));\n  }\n\n  // Scenario: Once the buffer pool is full, we should not be able to create any new pages.\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < FRAMES; i++) {\n    const auto pid = bpm->NewPage();\n    const auto fail = bpm->CheckedWritePage(pid);\n    ASSERT_FALSE(fail.has_value());\n  }\n\n  // Scenario: Drop the first 5 pages to unpin them.\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < FRAMES / 2; i++) {\n    const auto pid = pages[0].GetPageId();\n    EXPECT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pid));\n    pages.erase(pages.begin());\n    EXPECT_EQ(0, bpm->GetPinCount(pid));\n  }\n\n  // Scenario: All of the pin counts of the pages we haven't dropped yet should still be 1.\n  for (const auto &page : pages) {\n    const auto pid = page.GetPageId();\n    EXPECT_EQ(1, bpm->GetPinCount(pid));\n  }\n\n  // Scenario: After unpinning pages {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}, we should be able to create 4 new pages and bring them into\n  // memory. Bringing those 4 pages into memory should evict the first 4 pages {1, 2, 3, 4} because of LRU.\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < ((FRAMES / 2) - 1); i++) {\n    const auto pid = bpm->NewPage();\n    auto page = bpm->WritePage(pid);\n    pages.push_back(std::move(page));\n  }\n\n  // Scenario: There should be one frame available, and we should be able to fetch the data we wrote a while ago.\n  {\n    const auto original_page = bpm->ReadPage(pid0);\n    EXPECT_STREQ(original_page.GetData(), hello.c_str());\n  }\n\n  // Scenario: Once we unpin page 0 and then make a new page, all the buffer pages should now be pinned. Fetching page 0\n  // again should fail.\n  const auto last_pid = bpm->NewPage();\n  const auto last_page = bpm->ReadPage(last_pid);\n\n  const auto fail = bpm->CheckedReadPage(pid0);\n  ASSERT_FALSE(fail.has_value());\n\n  // Shutdown the disk manager and remove the temporary file we created.\n  disk_manager->ShutDown();\n  remove(db_fname);\n}\n\nTEST(BufferPoolManagerTest, DISABLED_PageAccessTest) {\n  const size_t rounds = 50;\n\n  auto disk_manager = std::make_shared<DiskManager>(db_fname);\n  auto bpm = std::make_shared<BufferPoolManager>(1, disk_manager.get());\n\n  const auto pid = bpm->NewPage();\n  char buf[BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE];\n\n  auto thread = std::thread([&]() {\n    // The writer can keep writing to the same page.\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < rounds; i++) {\n      std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(5));\n      auto guard = bpm->WritePage(pid);\n      CopyString(guard.GetDataMut(), std::to_string(i));\n    }\n  });\n\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < rounds; i++) {\n    // Wait for a bit before taking the latch, allowing the writer to write some stuff.\n    std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(10));\n\n    // While we are reading, nobody should be able to modify the data.\n    const auto guard = bpm->ReadPage(pid);\n\n    // Save the data we observe.\n    memcpy(buf, guard.GetData(), BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE);\n\n    // Sleep for a bit. If latching is working properly, nothing should be writing to the page.\n    std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(10));\n\n    // Check that the data is unmodified.\n    EXPECT_STREQ(guard.GetData(), buf);\n  }\n\n  thread.join();\n}\n\nTEST(BufferPoolManagerTest, DISABLED_ContentionTest) {\n  auto disk_manager = std::make_shared<DiskManager>(db_fname);\n  auto bpm = std::make_shared<BufferPoolManager>(FRAMES, disk_manager.get());\n\n  const size_t rounds = 100000;\n\n  const auto pid = bpm->NewPage();\n\n  auto thread1 = std::thread([&]() {\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < rounds; i++) {\n      auto guard = bpm->WritePage(pid);\n      CopyString(guard.GetDataMut(), std::to_string(i));\n    }\n  });\n\n  auto thread2 = std::thread([&]() {\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < rounds; i++) {\n      auto guard = bpm->WritePage(pid);\n      CopyString(guard.GetDataMut(), std::to_string(i));\n    }\n  });\n\n  auto thread3 = std::thread([&]() {\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < rounds; i++) {\n      auto guard = bpm->WritePage(pid);\n      CopyString(guard.GetDataMut(), std::to_string(i));\n    }\n  });\n\n  auto thread4 = std::thread([&]() {\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < rounds; i++) {\n      auto guard = bpm->WritePage(pid);\n      CopyString(guard.GetDataMut(), std::to_string(i));\n    }\n  });\n\n  thread3.join();\n  thread2.join();\n  thread4.join();\n  thread1.join();\n}\n\nTEST(BufferPoolManagerTest, DISABLED_DeadlockTest) {\n  auto disk_manager = std::make_shared<DiskManager>(db_fname);\n  auto bpm = std::make_shared<BufferPoolManager>(FRAMES, disk_manager.get());\n\n  const auto pid0 = bpm->NewPage();\n  const auto pid1 = bpm->NewPage();\n\n  auto guard0 = bpm->WritePage(pid0);\n\n  // A crude way of synchronizing threads, but works for this small case.\n  std::atomic<bool> start = false;\n\n  auto child = std::thread([&]() {\n    // Acknowledge that we can begin the test.\n    start.store(true);\n\n    // Attempt to write to page 0.\n    const auto guard0 = bpm->WritePage(pid0);\n  });\n\n  // Wait for the other thread to begin before we start the test.\n  while (!start.load()) {\n  }\n\n  // Make the other thread wait for a bit.\n  // This mimics the main thread doing some work while holding the write latch on page 0.\n  std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(1000));\n\n  // If your latching mechanism is incorrect, the next line of code will deadlock.\n  // Think about what might happen if you hold a certain \"all-encompassing\" latch for too long...\n\n  // While holding page 0, take the latch on page 1.\n  const auto guard1 = bpm->WritePage(pid1);\n\n  // Let the child thread have the page 0 since we're done with it.\n  guard0.Drop();\n\n  child.join();\n}\n\nTEST(BufferPoolManagerTest, DISABLED_EvictableTest) {\n  // Test if the evictable status of a frame is always correct.\n  const size_t rounds = 1000;\n  const size_t num_readers = 8;\n\n  auto disk_manager = std::make_shared<DiskManager>(db_fname);\n  // Only allocate one frame of memory to the buffer pool manager.\n  auto bpm = std::make_shared<BufferPoolManager>(1, disk_manager.get());\n\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < rounds; i++) {\n    std::mutex mutex;\n    std::condition_variable cv;\n\n    // This signal tells the readers that they can start reading after the main thread has already taken the read latch.\n    bool signal = false;\n\n    // This page will be loaded into the only available frame.\n    const auto winner_pid = bpm->NewPage();\n    // We will attempt to load this page into the occupied frame, and it should fail every time.\n    const auto loser_pid = bpm->NewPage();\n\n    std::vector<std::thread> readers;\n    for (size_t j = 0; j < num_readers; j++) {\n      readers.emplace_back([&]() {\n        std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex);\n\n        // Wait until the main thread has taken a read latch on the page.\n        while (!signal) {\n          cv.wait(lock);\n        }\n\n        // Read the page in shared mode.\n        const auto read_guard = bpm->ReadPage(winner_pid);\n\n        // Since the only frame is pinned, no thread should be able to bring in a new page.\n        ASSERT_FALSE(bpm->CheckedReadPage(loser_pid).has_value());\n      });\n    }\n\n    std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex);\n\n    if (i % 2 == 0) {\n      // Take the read latch on the page and pin it.\n      auto read_guard = bpm->ReadPage(winner_pid);\n\n      // Wake up all of the readers.\n      signal = true;\n      cv.notify_all();\n      lock.unlock();\n\n      // Allow other threads to read.\n      read_guard.Drop();\n    } else {\n      // Take the read latch on the page and pin it.\n      auto write_guard = bpm->WritePage(winner_pid);\n\n      // Wake up all of the readers.\n      signal = true;\n      cv.notify_all();\n      lock.unlock();\n\n      // Allow other threads to read.\n      write_guard.Drop();\n    }\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < num_readers; i++) {\n      readers[i].join();\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/buffer_pool_manager_test.cpp",
    "numLines": 432,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 432
  },
  "type": "text"
}

}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// :bustub-keep-private:\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include <optional>\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief a new ArcReplacer, with lists initialized to be empty and target size to 0\n * @param num_frames the maximum number of frames the ArcReplacer will be required to cache\n */\nArcReplacer::ArcReplacer(size_t num_frames) : replacer_size_(num_frames) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ into its corresponding ghost list\n * according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Record access to a frame, adjusting ARC bookkeeping accordingly\n * by bring the accessed page to the front of mfu_ if it exists in any of the lists\n * or the front of mru_ if it does not.\n *\n * Performs the operations EXCEPT REPLACE described in original paper, which is\n * handled by `Evict()`.\n *\n * Consider the following four cases, handle accordingly:\n * 1. Access hits mru_ or mfu_\n * 2/3. Access hits mru_ghost_ / mfu_ghost_\n * 4. Access misses all the lists\n *\n * This routine performs all changes to the four lists as preperation\n * for `Evict()` to simply find and evict a victim into ghost lists.\n *\n * Note that frame_id is used as identifier for alive pages and\n * page_id is used as identifier for the ghost pages, since page_id is\n * the unique identifier to the page after it's dead.\n * Using page_id for alive pages should be the same since it's one to one mapping,\n * but using frame_id is slightly more intuitive.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame that received a new access.\n * @param page_id id of page that is mapped to the frame.\n * @param access_type type of access that was received. This parameter is only needed for\n * leaderboard tests.\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Toggle whether a frame is evictable or non-evictable. This function also\n * controls replacer's size. Note that size is equal to number of evictable entries.\n *\n * If a frame was previously evictable and is to be set to non-evictable, then size should\n * decrement. If a frame was previously non-evictable and is to be set to evictable,\n * then size should increment.\n *\n * If frame id is invalid, throw an exception or abort the process.\n *\n * For other scenarios, this function should terminate without modifying anything.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame whose 'evictable' status will be modified\n * @param set_evictable whether the given frame is evictable or not\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Remove an evictable frame from replacer.\n * This function should also decrement replacer's size if removal is successful.\n *\n * Note that this is different from evicting a frame, which always remove the frame\n * decided by the ARC algorithm.\n *\n * If Remove is called on a non-evictable frame, throw an exception or abort the\n * process.\n *\n * If specified frame is not found, directly return from this function.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame to be removed\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Return replacer's size, which tracks the number of evictable frames.\n *\n * @return size_t\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t { return 0; }\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
    "numLines": 127,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 127
  },
  "type": "text"

{
  "file": {
    "content": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// config.h\n//\n// Identification: src/include/common/config.h\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#pragma once\n\n#include <atomic>\n#include <chrono>  // NOLINT\n#include <cstdint>\n\n#define DISABLE_LOCK_MANAGER\n#define DISABLE_CHECKPOINT_MANAGER\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/** Cycle detection is performed every CYCLE_DETECTION_INTERVAL milliseconds. */\nextern std::chrono::milliseconds cycle_detection_interval;\n\n/** True if logging should be enabled, false otherwise. */\nextern std::atomic<bool> enable_logging;\n\n/** If ENABLE_LOGGING is true, the log should be flushed to disk every LOG_TIMEOUT. */\nextern std::chrono::duration<int64_t> log_timeout;\n\nstatic constexpr int INVALID_FRAME_ID = -1;  // invalid frame id\nstatic constexpr int INVALID_PAGE_ID = -1;   // invalid page id\nstatic constexpr int INVALID_TXN_ID = -1;    // invalid transaction id\nstatic constexpr int INVALID_LSN = -1;       // invalid log sequence number\n\nstatic constexpr int BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE = 8192;                                        // size of a data page in byte\nstatic constexpr int BUFFER_POOL_SIZE = 128;                                         // size of buffer pool\nstatic constexpr int DEFAULT_DB_IO_SIZE = 16;                                        // starting size of file on disk\nstatic constexpr int LOG_BUFFER_SIZE = ((BUFFER_POOL_SIZE + 1) * BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE);  // size of a log buffer in byte\nstatic constexpr int BUCKET_SIZE = 50;                                               // size of extendible hash bucket\nstatic constexpr int LRUK_REPLACER_K = 10;                                           // backward k-distance for lru-k\nstatic constexpr int BUSTUB_BATCH_SIZE = 20;  // number of tuples processed in a batch\n\nusing frame_id_t = int32_t;    // frame id type\nusing page_id_t = int32_t;     // page id type\nusing txn_id_t = int64_t;      // transaction id type\nusing lsn_t = int32_t;         // log sequence number type\nusing slot_offset_t = size_t;  // slot offset type\nusing oid_t = uint16_t;\n\nconst txn_id_t TXN_START_ID = 1LL << 62;  // first txn id\n\nstatic constexpr int VARCHAR_DEFAULT_LENGTH = 128;  // default length for varchar when constructing the column\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/include/common/config.h",
    "numLines": 58,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 58

  "file": {
    "content": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// macros.h\n//\n// Identification: src/include/common/macros.h\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#pragma once\n\n#include <cassert>\n#include <exception>\n#include <iostream>\n#include <sstream>\n#include <stdexcept>\n\nnamespace bustub {  // NOLINT\n\n#define BUSTUB_ASSERT(expr, message) assert((expr) && (message))\n\nnamespace internal {  // NOLINT\n\n// An internal stream, used to take C++ stream-style parameters for display, and exit the program with `std::abort`.\nclass LogFatalStream {\n public:\n  LogFatalStream(const char *file, int line) : file_(file), line_(line) {}\n\n  ~LogFatalStream() {\n    std::cerr << file_ << \":\" << line_ << \": \" << log_stream_.str() << std::endl;\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  template <typename T>\n  auto operator<<(const T &val) -> LogFatalStream & {\n    log_stream_ << val;\n    return *this;\n  }\n\n private:\n  const char *file_;\n  int line_;\n  std::ostringstream log_stream_;\n};\n\n}  // namespace internal\n\n// A macro which checks `expr` value and performs assert.\n// Different from `BUSTUB_ASSERT`, it takes stream-style parameters.\n#define BUSTUB_ASSERT_AND_LOG(expr)                       /*NOLINT*/ \\\n  if (bool val = (expr); !val) internal::LogFatalStream { /*NOLINT*/ \\\n      __FILE__, __LINE__                                  /*NOLINT*/ \\\n    }\n\n#define UNIMPLEMENTED(message) throw std::logic_error(message)\n\n#define BUSTUB_ENSURE(expr, message)                  \\\n  if (!(expr)) {                                      \\\n    std::cerr << \"ERROR: \" << (message) << std::endl; \\\n    std::terminate();                                 \\\n  }\n\n#define UNREACHABLE(message) throw std::logic_error(message)\n\n// Macros to disable copying and moving\n#define DISALLOW_COPY(cname)                                    \\\n  cname(const cname &) = delete;                   /* NOLINT */ \\\n  auto operator=(const cname &)->cname & = delete; /* NOLINT */\n\n#define DISALLOW_MOVE(cname)                               \\\n  cname(cname &&) = delete;                   /* NOLINT */ \\\n  auto operator=(cname &&)->cname & = delete; /* NOLINT */\n\n#define DISALLOW_COPY_AND_MOVE(cname) \\\n  DISALLOW_COPY(cname);               \\\n  DISALLOW_MOVE(cname);\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/include/common/macros.h",

45. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/arc_replacer_test.cpp"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer_test.cpp\n//\n// Identification: test/buffer/arc_replacer_test.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n/**\n * arc_replacer_test.cpp\n */\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n\n#include \"gtest/gtest.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\nTEST(ArcReplacerTest, DISABLED_SampleTest) {\n  // for the sake of simplicity\n  // we use (a, fb) to notate page a on frame b,\n  // (a, _) to mark ghost page with page id a\n  // p(a, fb) to mark pinned page a on frame b\n  // we use [<-mru_ghost-][<-mru-]![-mfu->][->mfu_ghost->] p=x\n  // to denote the 4 lists where pages closer to ! is are more fresh\n  // and record the mru target size as x\n  ArcReplacer arc_replacer(7);\n  // Add six frames to the replacer.\n  // We set frame 6 as non-evictable. These pages all go to mru list\n  // We now have frames [][(1,f1), (2,f2), (3,f3), (4,f4), (5,f5), p(6,f6)]![][]\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 1);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 2);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 3);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(4, 4);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(5, 5);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(6, 6);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(4, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(5, true);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(6, false);\n\n  // The size of the replacer is the number of frames that can be evicted, _not_ the total number of frames entered.\n  ASSERT_EQ(5, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Record an access for frame 1. Now frame 1 goes into mfu list\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 1);\n  // Now [][(2,f2), (3,f3), (4,f4), (5,f5), p(6,f6)]![(1,f1)][] p=0\n  //\n  // Now Evict three pages from the replacer.\n  // Since target size is still 0, mru side should be evicted\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(4, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Now [(2,_), (3,_), (4,_)][(5,f5), p(6,f6)]![(1,f1)][] p=0\n\n  // Insert new page 7 on frame 2, this should NOT be a hit on ghost\n  // list since we've never seen page 7, this goes into mru list\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 7);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  // Insert page 2 on frame 3, this should be a hit on mru ghost list\n  // since we've just evicted page 2, this goes into mfu list\n  // also target size should be bumped up by 1, since mru ghost has\n  // size 3 and mfu ghost has size 0\n  // starting with x is pageid on ghost list, starting with _ is pinned\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 2);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  // Now [(3,_), (4,_)][(5,f5), p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(2,f3), (1,f1)][] p=1\n  ASSERT_EQ(4, arc_replacer.Size());\n\n  // Continue to insert page 3 on frame 4 and page 4 on frame 7\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(4, 3);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(4, true);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(7, 4);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(7, true);\n  // Now [][(5,f5), p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3), (1,f1)][] p=3\n  ASSERT_EQ(6, arc_replacer.Size());\n\n  // Evict an entry, now target size is 3, we still evict from mru\n  ASSERT_EQ(5, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(5,_)][p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3), (1,f1)][] p=3\n  // Evict another entry, this time mru is smaller than target,\n  // mfu is victimized\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(5,_)][p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3)][(1,_)] p=3\n\n  // Make another access to page 1 on frame 5, now page 1 is back to mfu\n  // with a different frame, also p is adjusted down by 1/1=1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(5, 1);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(5, true);\n  // Now [(5,_)][p(6,f6), (7,f2)]![(1,f5), (4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3)][] p=2\n  ASSERT_EQ(5, arc_replacer.Size());\n\n  // We evict again, this time target size is 2, we evict from mru,\n  // note that page 6 is pinned. Victim is page 7\n  // Now [(5,_), (7,_)][p(6,f6)]![(1,f5), (4,f7), (3,f4), (2,f3)][] p=2\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n}\n\nTEST(ArcReplacerTest, DISABLED_SampleTest2) {\n  // Test a smaller capacity\n  ArcReplacer arc_replacer(3);\n  // Fill up the replacer\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 1);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 2);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 3);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Now [][(1,f1), (2,f2), (3,f3)]![][] p=0\n  // Evict all pages\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(0, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Now [(1,_), (2,_), (3,_)][]![][] p=0\n\n  // Insert a new page 4 with frame 3. This is case 4A\n  // and ghost pages 1 should be driven out\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 4);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  // Now [(2,_), (3,_)][(4,f3)]![][] p=0\n\n  // Access page 1 on frame 2, it should NOT be a hit on\n  // the ghost list. Ghost page 2 should be driven out\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 1);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Size());\n  // Now [(3,_)][(4,f3), (1,f2)]![][] p=0\n\n  // Access page 3 with frame 1, this should be a ghost hit,\n  // page 3 is placed on mfu and target size is bumped up by 1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 3);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  // Now [][(4,f3), (1,f2)]![(3,f1)][] p=1\n\n  // Make some more ghosts by evicting all pages again\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(4,_), (1,_)][]![][(3,_)] p=1\n\n  // Let's make even more ghost to fill the list to \"full\"\n  // Insert page 1 again so it goes to mfu side,\n  // target is bumped up by 1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 1);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  // Now [(4,_)][]![(1,f1)][(3,_)] p=2\n\n  // Insert page 4 again so it goes to mfu side,\n  // target is bumped up by 1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 4);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  // Now [][]![(4,f2),(1,f1)][(3,_)] p=3\n\n  // Now insert and evict one new page at a time\n  // Insert page 5 and evict, since target size is 3,\n  // should victimize page 1\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 5);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [][(5,f3)]![(4,f2)][(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n  // Insert page 6 and evict, notice target size is 3,\n  // so page 4 gets evicted\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 6);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [][(5,f3),(6,f1)]![(4,_),(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n  // Insert page 7 and evict, notice target size is 3,\n  // so page 5 gets evicted\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(2, 7);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(2, true);\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(5,_)][(6,f1),(7,f2)]![][(4,_),(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n\n  // Now the list is full! reaching 2*capacity\n  // adjust page 5 to mfu list\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 5);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  // Now [][(6,f1),(7,f2)]![(5,f3)][(4,_),(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n\n  // Now evict, target should be mfu\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [][(6,f1),(7,f2)]![][(5,_),(4,_),(1,_),(3,_)] p=3\n\n  // Now mru and mru_ghost together has\n  // less than 3 records. When inserting a new page 2\n  // this should be case 4B and\n  // four lists total size equals 2 * capacity case,\n  // So mfu ghost will be shrinked\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(3, 2);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(3, true);\n  // Now [][(6,f1),(7,f2),(2,f3)]![][(5,_),(4,_),(1,_)] p=3\n\n  // Evict a page 6\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  // Now [(6,_)][(7,f2),(2,f3)]![][(5,_),(4,_),(1,_)] p=3\n  // And access page 3 who was removed\n  // then this is case 4A, ghost page 6 will be removed\n  arc_replacer.RecordAccess(1, 3);\n  arc_replacer.SetEvictable(1, true);\n  // Now [][(7,f2),(2,f3),(3,f1)]![][(5,_),(4,_),(1,_)] p=3\n\n  // Finally we evict all pages and see if the order is right,\n  // note that target size is 3\n  ASSERT_EQ(2, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(3, arc_replacer.Evict());\n  ASSERT_EQ(1, arc_replacer.Evict());\n}\n\n// Feel free to write more tests!\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/arc_replacer_test.cpp",
    "numLines": 220,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 220
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
  "new_string": "/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list\n  auto evict_from_list = [this](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,\n                                std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {\n      frame_id_t frame_id = *it;\n      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {\n        // Found an evictable frame\n        page_id_t page_id = status_it->second->page_id_;\n        ArcStatus status = status_it->second->arc_status_;\n\n        // Remove from alive list\n        list.erase(it);\n        alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n        curr_size_--;\n\n        // Add to corresponding ghost list\n        auto ghost_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(\n            page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false,\n            (status == ArcStatus::MRU) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST);\n        ghost_list.push_back(page_id);\n        ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status;\n\n        // Limit ghost list size\n        while (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mru_ghost_.front();\n            mru_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n        while (mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();\n            mfu_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n\n        return frame_id;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };\n\n  // Determine which list to evict from based on target size\n  // If mru_.size() > mru_target_size_, evict from mru\n  // Otherwise evict from mfu\n  size_t mru_alive_size = mru_.size();\n\n  if (mru_alive_size > mru_target_size_) {\n    // Try to evict from mru first\n    auto result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n    if (result.has_value()) {\n      return result;\n    }\n    // If mru has no evictable frames, try mfu\n    return evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  }\n  // Try to evict from mfu first\n  auto result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  if (result.has_value()) {\n    return result;\n  }\n  // If mfu has no evictable frames, try mru\n  return evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n}",
  "old_string": "/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }",
  "replace_all": false
}

{
  "file": {
    "content": "// :bustub-keep-private:\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include <optional>\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief a new ArcReplacer, with lists initialized to be empty and target size to 0\n * @param num_frames the maximum number of frames the ArcReplacer will be required to cache\n */\nArcReplacer::ArcReplacer(size_t num_frames) : replacer_size_(num_frames) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ into its corresponding ghost list\n * according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Record access to a frame, adjusting ARC bookkeeping accordingly\n * by bring the accessed page to the front of mfu_ if it exists in any of the lists\n * or the front of mru_ if it does not.\n *\n * Performs the operations EXCEPT REPLACE described in original paper, which is\n * handled by `Evict()`.\n *\n * Consider the following four cases, handle accordingly:\n * 1. Access hits mru_ or mfu_\n * 2/3. Access hits mru_ghost_ / mfu_ghost_\n * 4. Access misses all the lists\n *\n * This routine performs all changes to the four lists as preperation\n * for `Evict()` to simply find and evict a victim into ghost lists.\n *\n * Note that frame_id is used as identifier for alive pages and\n * page_id is used as identifier for the ghost pages, since page_id is\n * the unique identifier to the page after it's dead.\n * Using page_id for alive pages should be the same since it's one to one mapping,\n * but using frame_id is slightly more intuitive.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame that received a new access.\n * @param page_id id of page that is mapped to the frame.\n * @param access_type type of access that was received. This parameter is only needed for\n * leaderboard tests.\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Toggle whether a frame is evictable or non-evictable. This function also\n * controls replacer's size. Note that size is equal to number of evictable entries.\n *\n * If a frame was previously evictable and is to be set to non-evictable, then size should\n * decrement. If a frame was previously non-evictable and is to be set to evictable,\n * then size should increment.\n *\n * If frame id is invalid, throw an exception or abort the process.\n *\n * For other scenarios, this function should terminate without modifying anything.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame whose 'evictable' status will be modified\n * @param set_evictable whether the given frame is evictable or not\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Remove an evictable frame from replacer.\n * This function should also decrement replacer's size if removal is successful.\n *\n * Note that this is different from evicting a frame, which always remove the frame\n * decided by the ARC algorithm.\n *\n * If Remove is called on a non-evictable frame, throw an exception or abort the\n * process.\n *\n * If specified frame is not found, directly return from this function.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame to be removed\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Return replacer's size, which tracks the number of evictable frames.\n *\n * @return size_t\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t { return 0; }\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
    "numLines": 127,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 127
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "new_string": "auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list\n  auto evict_from_list = [this](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,\n                                std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {\n      frame_id_t frame_id = *it;\n      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {\n        // Found an evictable frame\n        page_id_t page_id = status_it->second->page_id_;\n        ArcStatus status = status_it->second->arc_status_;\n\n        // Remove from alive list\n        list.erase(it);\n        alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n        curr_size_--;\n\n        // Add to corresponding ghost list\n        auto ghost_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(\n            page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false,\n            (status == ArcStatus::MRU) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST);\n        ghost_list.push_back(page_id);\n        ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status;\n\n        // Limit ghost list size to ensure total size <= 2 * replacer_size_\n        while (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mru_ghost_.front();\n            mru_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n        while (mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();\n            mfu_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n\n        return frame_id;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };\n\n  // Determine which list to evict from based on target size\n  // If mru_.size() > mru_target_size_, evict from mru\n  // Otherwise evict from mfu\n  size_t mru_alive_size = mru_.size();\n\n  if (mru_alive_size > mru_target_size_) {\n    // Try to evict from mru first\n    auto result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n    if (result.has_value()) {\n      return result;\n    }\n    // If mru has no evictable frames, try mfu\n    return evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  }\n  // Try to evict from mfu first\n  auto result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  if (result.has_value()) {\n    return result;\n  }\n  // If mfu has no evictable frames, try mru\n  return evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n}",

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
  "newString": "auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list\n  auto evict_from_list = [this](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,\n                                std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {\n      frame_id_t frame_id = *it;\n      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {\n        // Found an evictable frame\n        page_id_t page_id = status_it->second->page_id_;\n        ArcStatus status = status_it->second->arc_status_;\n\n        // Remove from alive list\n        list.erase(it);\n        alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n        curr_size_--;\n\n        // Add to corresponding ghost list\n        auto ghost_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(\n            page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false,\n            (status == ArcStatus::MRU) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST);\n        ghost_list.push_back(page_id);\n        ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status;\n\n        // Limit ghost list size to ensure total size <= 2 * replacer_size_\n        while (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mru_ghost_.front();\n            mru_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n        while (mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();\n            mfu_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n\n        return frame_id;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };\n\n  // Determine which list to evict from based on target size\n  // If mru_.size() > mru_target_size_, evict from mru\n  // Otherwise evict from mfu\n  size_t mru_alive_size = mru_.size();\n\n  if (mru_alive_size > mru_target_size_) {\n    // Try to evict from mru first\n    auto result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n    if (result.has_value()) {\n      return result;\n    }\n    // If mru has no evictable frames, try mfu\n    return evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  }\n  // Try to evict from mfu first\n  auto result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  if (result.has_value()) {\n    return result;\n  }\n  // If mfu has no evictable frames, try mru\n  return evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n}",
  "oldString": "auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }",
  "originalFile": "// :bustub-keep-private:\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include <optional>\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief a new ArcReplacer, with lists initialized to be empty and target size to 0\n * @param num_frames the maximum number of frames the ArcReplacer will be required to cache\n */\nArcReplacer::ArcReplacer(size_t num_frames) : replacer_size_(num_frames) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ into its corresponding ghost list\n * according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Record access to a frame, adjusting ARC bookkeeping accordingly\n * by bring the accessed page to the front of mfu_ if it exists in any of the lists\n * or the front of mru_ if it does not.\n *\n * Performs the operations EXCEPT REPLACE described in original paper, which is\n * handled by `Evict()`.\n *\n * Consider the following four cases, handle accordingly:\n * 1. Access hits mru_ or mfu_\n * 2/3. Access hits mru_ghost_ / mfu_ghost_\n * 4. Access misses all the lists\n *\n * This routine performs all changes to the four lists as preperation\n * for `Evict()` to simply find and evict a victim into ghost lists.\n *\n * Note that frame_id is used as identifier for alive pages and\n * page_id is used as identifier for the ghost pages, since page_id is\n * the unique identifier to the page after it's dead.\n * Using page_id for alive pages should be the same since it's one to one mapping,\n * but using frame_id is slightly more intuitive.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame that received a new access.\n * @param page_id id of page that is mapped to the frame.\n * @param access_type type of access that was received. This parameter is only needed for\n * leaderboard tests.\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Toggle whether a frame is evictable or non-evictable. This function also\n * controls replacer's size. Note that size is equal to number of evictable entries.\n *\n * If a frame was previously evictable and is to be set to non-evictable, then size should\n * decrement. If a frame was previously non-evictable and is to be set to evictable,\n * then size should increment.\n *\n * If frame id is invalid, throw an exception or abort the process.\n *\n * For other scenarios, this function should terminate without modifying anything.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame whose 'evictable' status will be modified\n * @param set_evictable whether the given frame is evictable or not\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Remove an evictable frame from replacer.\n * This function should also decrement replacer's size if removal is successful.\n *\n * Note that this is different from evicting a frame, which always remove the frame\n * decided by the ARC algorithm.\n *\n * If Remove is called on a non-evictable frame, throw an exception or abort the\n * process.\n *\n * If specified frame is not found, directly return from this function.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame to be removed\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Return replacer's size, which tracks the number of evictable frames.\n *\n * @return size_t\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t { return 0; }\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [

        "  * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict",
        "  */",
        "-auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }",
        "+auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {",
        "+  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);",
        " ",
        "+  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list",
        "+  auto evict_from_list = [this](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,",
        "+                                std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {",
        "+    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {",
        "+      frame_id_t frame_id = *it;",
        "+      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);",
        "+      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {",
        "+        // Found an evictable frame",
        "+        page_id_t page_id = status_it->second->page_id_;",
        "+        ArcStatus status = status_it->second->arc_status_;",
        "+",
        "+        // Remove from alive list",
        "+        list.erase(it);",
        "+        alive_map_.erase(frame_id);",
        "+        curr_size_--;",
        "+",
        "+        // Add to corresponding ghost list",
        "+        auto ghost_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(",
        "+            page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false,",
        "+            (status == ArcStatus::MRU) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST);",
        "+        ghost_list.push_back(page_id);",
        "+        ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status;",
        "+",
        "+        // Limit ghost list size to ensure total size <= 2 * replacer_size_",

        "+            mru_ghost_.pop_front();",
        "+            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);",
        "+          }",
        "+        }",
        "+        while (mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {",

        "+            page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();",
        "+            mfu_ghost_.pop_front();",
        "+            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);",
        "+          }",
        "+        }",
        "+",
        "+        return frame_id;",
        "+      }",
        "+    }",
        "+    return std::nullopt;",
        "+  };",

        "+  // Determine which list to evict from based on target size",
        "+  // If mru_.size() > mru_target_size_, evict from mru",
        "+  // Otherwise evict from mfu",
        "+  size_t mru_alive_size = mru_.size();",
        "+",
        "+  if (mru_alive_size > mru_target_size_) {",
        "+    // Try to evict from mru first",
        "+    auto result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);",
        "+    if (result.has_value()) {",
        "+      return result;",
        "+    }",
        "+    // If mru has no evictable frames, try mfu",
        "+    return evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);",
        "+  }",
        "+  // Try to evict from mfu first",
        "+  auto result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);",
        "+  if (result.has_value()) {",
        "+    return result;",
        "+  }",
        "+  // If mfu has no evictable frames, try mru",
        "+  return evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);",

49. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
  "new_string": "void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Case 1: Access hits mru_ or mfu_ (frame is already in alive_map)\n  auto alive_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n  if (alive_it != alive_map_.end()) {\n    auto &status = alive_it->second;\n    // Move to mfu (frequent use)\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      // Remove from mru list\n      mru_.remove(frame_id);\n      // Add to back of mfu list\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n      status->arc_status_ = ArcStatus::MFU;\n    } else {\n      // Already in mfu, move to back (most recently used)\n      mfu_.remove(frame_id);\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Case 2: Access hits mru_ghost_\n  auto ghost_it = ghost_map_.find(page_id);\n  if (ghost_it != ghost_map_.end()) {\n    auto &ghost_status = ghost_it->second;\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST) {\n      // Increase target size: delta = max(1, |mfu_ghost| / |mru_ghost|)\n      size_t delta = std::max(static_cast<size_t>(1), mfu_ghost_.size() / std::max(mru_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)));\n      mru_target_size_ = std::min(mru_target_size_ + delta, replacer_size_);\n\n      // Remove from ghost list\n      mru_ghost_.remove(page_id);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to mfu list\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n    // Case 3: Access hits mfu_ghost_\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST) {\n      // Decrease target size: delta = max(1, |mru_ghost| / |mfu_ghost|)\n      size_t delta = std::max(static_cast<size_t>(1), mru_ghost_.size() / std::max(mfu_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)));\n      if (mru_target_size_ >= delta) {\n        mru_target_size_ -= delta;\n      } else {\n        mru_target_size_ = 0;\n      }\n\n      // Remove from ghost list\n      mfu_ghost_.remove(page_id);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to mfu list\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists - new page\n  // Case 4A: mru_ + mru_ghost_ >= replacer_size_\n  if (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() >= replacer_size_) {\n    if (mru_.size() < replacer_size_) {\n      // Remove oldest from mru_ghost_\n      if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n        page_id_t old_page = mru_ghost_.front();\n        mru_ghost_.pop_front();\n        ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    // Case 4B: mru_ + mru_ghost_ < replacer_size_\n    // Total size might equal 2 * replacer_size_, need to remove from mfu_ghost_\n    size_t total_size = mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() + mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size();\n    if (total_size >= 2 * replacer_size_) {\n      if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n        page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();\n        mfu_ghost_.pop_front();\n        ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Insert new page into mru list\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  mru_.push_back(frame_id);\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}",
  "old_string": "void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {}",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "newString": "void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Case 1: Access hits mru_ or mfu_ (frame is already in alive_map)\n  auto alive_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n  if (alive_it != alive_map_.end()) {\n    auto &status = alive_it->second;\n    // Move to mfu (frequent use)\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      // Remove from mru list\n      mru_.remove(frame_id);\n      // Add to back of mfu list\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n      status->arc_status_
