  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\"\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The constructor for a `FrameHeader` that initializes all fields to default values.\n *\n * See the documentation for `FrameHeader` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * @param frame_id The frame ID / index of the frame we are creating a header for.\n */\nFrameHeader::FrameHeader(frame_id_t frame_id) : frame_id_(frame_id), data_(BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE, 0) { Reset(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw const pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return const char* A pointer to immutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetData() const -> const char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw mutable pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return char* A pointer to mutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetDataMut() -> char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Resets a `FrameHeader`'s member fields.\n */\nvoid FrameHeader::Reset() {\n  std::fill(data_.begin(), data_.end(), 0);\n  pin_count_.store(0);\n  is_dirty_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Creates a new `BufferPoolManager` instance and initializes all fields.\n *\n * See the documentation for `BufferPoolManager` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * We have implemented the constructor for you in a way that makes sense with our reference solution. You are free to\n * change anything you would like here if it doesn't fit with you implementation.\n *\n * Be warned, though! If you stray too far away from our guidance, it will be much harder for us to help you. Our\n * recommendation would be to first implement the buffer pool manager using the stepping stones we have provided.\n *\n * Once you have a fully working solution (all Gradescope test cases pass), then you can try more interesting things!\n *\n * @param num_frames The size of the buffer pool.\n * @param disk_manager The disk manager.\n * @param log_manager The log manager. Please ignore this for P1.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::BufferPoolManager(size_t num_frames, DiskManager *disk_manager, LogManager *log_manager)\n    : num_frames_(num_frames),\n      next_page_id_(0),\n      bpm_latch_(std::make_shared<std::mutex>()),\n      replacer_(std::make_shared<ArcReplacer>(num_frames)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::make_shared<DiskScheduler>(disk_manager)),\n      log_manager_(log_manager) {\n  // Not strictly necessary...\n  std::scoped_lock latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Initialize the monotonically increasing counter at 0.\n  next_page_id_.store(0);\n\n  // Allocate all of the in-memory frames up front.\n  frames_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // The page table should have exactly `num_frames_` slots, corresponding to exactly `num_frames_` frames.\n  page_table_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // Initialize all of the frame headers, and fill the free frame list with all possible frame IDs (since all frames are\n  // initially free).\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < num_frames_; i++) {\n    frames_.push_back(std::make_shared<FrameHeader>(i));\n    free_frames_.push_back(static_cast<int>(i));\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Destroys the `BufferPoolManager`, freeing up all memory that the buffer pool was using.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::~BufferPoolManager() = default;\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns the number of frames that this buffer pool manages.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::Size() const -> size_t { return num_frames_; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Allocates a new page on disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You will maintain a thread-safe, monotonically increasing counter in the form of a `std::atomic<page_id_t>`.\n * See the documentation on [atomics](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic) for more information.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @return The page ID of the newly allocated page.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t {\n  // Atomically increment and return the page id\n  return next_page_id_.fetch_add(1);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Removes a page from the database, both on disk and in memory.\n *\n * If the page is pinned in the buffer pool, this function does nothing and returns `false`. Otherwise, this function\n * removes the page from both disk and memory (if it is still in the buffer pool), returning `true`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Think about all of the places that a page or a page's metadata could be, and use that to guide you on implementing\n * this function. You will probably want to implement this function _after_ you have implemented `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * You should call `DeallocatePage` in the disk scheduler to make the space available for new pages.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to delete.\n * @return `false` if the page exists but could not be deleted, `true` if the page didn't exist or deletion succeeded.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Check if page is in memory\n  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n  if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n    // Page not in memory, just deallocate from disk\n    disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n  auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n  // Check if the page is pinned\n  if (frame->pin_count_.load() > 0) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Remove from page table\n  page_table_.erase(it);\n\n  // Remove from replacer\n  replacer_->Remove(frame_id);\n\n  // Reset the frame\n  frame->Reset();\n\n  // Add frame back to free list\n  free_frames_.push_back(frame_id);\n\n  // Deallocate page from disk\n  disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional write-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can only be 1 `WritePageGuard` reading/writing a page at a time. This allows data access to be both immutable\n * and mutable, meaning the thread that owns the `WritePageGuard` is allowed to manipulate the page's data however they\n * want. If a user wants to have multiple threads reading the page at the same time, they must acquire a `ReadPageGuard`\n * with `CheckedReadPage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * There are three main cases that you will have to implement. The first two are relatively simple: one is when there is\n * plenty of available memory, and the other is when we don't actually need to perform any additional I/O. Think about\n * what exactly these two cases entail.\n *\n * The third case is the trickiest, and it is when we do not have any _easily_ available memory at our disposal. The\n * buffer pool is tasked with finding memory that it can use to bring in a page of memory, using the replacement\n * algorithm you implemented previously to find candidate frames for eviction.\n *\n * Once the buffer pool has identified a frame for eviction, several I/O operations may be necessary to bring in the\n * page of data we want into the frame.\n *\n * There is likely going to be a lot of shared code with `CheckedReadPage`, so you may find creating helper functions\n * useful.\n *\n * These two functions are the crux of this project, so we won't give you more hints than this. Good luck!\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<WritePageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `WritePageGuard` ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Check if page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      // Return the guard (lock will be acquired in constructor)\n      return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n    }\n\n    // Case 2: Page not in buffer pool, need to find a frame\n    // First, try to get a free frame\n    if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n      frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n      free_frames_.pop_front();\n    } else {\n      // Case 3: No free frames, need to evict\n      auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();\n      if (!evicted.has_value()) {\n        // Could not evict any frame\n        return std::nullopt;\n      }\n      frame_id = evicted.value();\n\n      // Find the page currently in this frame\n      for (auto &[pid, fid] : page_table_) {\n        if (fid == frame_id) {\n          // If dirty, flush to disk\n          auto &evicted_frame = frames_[frame_id];\n          if (evicted_frame->is_dirty_) {\n            auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n            auto future = promise.get_future();\n            std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n            requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, evicted_frame->GetDataMut(), pid, std::move(promise)});\n            disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n            future.get();\n          }\n          page_table_.erase(pid);\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Now we have a frame, use it for the new page\n    frame = frames_[frame_id];\n    frame->Reset();\n\n    // Read the page from disk\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    // Update page table\n    page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n    // Set pin count and record access\n    frame->pin_count_.store(1);\n    replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n  }\n\n  return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional read-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can be any number of `ReadPageGuard`s reading the same page of data at a time across different threads.\n * However, all data access must be immutable. If a user wants to mutate the page's data, they must acquire a\n * `WritePageGuard` with `CheckedWritePage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * See the implementation details of `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<ReadPageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `ReadPageGuard` ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Check if page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      // Return the guard (lock will be acquired in constructor)\n      return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n    }\n\n    // Case 2: Page not in buffer pool, need to find a frame\n    // First, try to get a free frame\n    if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n      frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n      free_frames_.pop_front();\n    } else {\n      // Case 3: No free frames, need to evict\n      auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();\n      if (!evicted.has_value()) {\n        // Could not evict any frame\n        return std::nullopt;\n      }\n      frame_id = evicted.value();\n\n      // Find the page currently in this frame\n      for (auto &[pid, fid] : page_table_) {\n        if (fid == frame_id) {\n          // If dirty, flush to disk\n          auto &evicted_frame = frames_[frame_id];\n          if (evicted_frame->is_dirty_) {\n            auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n            auto future = promise.get_future();\n            std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n            requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, evicted_frame->GetDataMut(), pid, std::move(promise)});\n            disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n            future.get();\n          }\n          page_table_.erase(pid);\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Now we have a frame, use it for the new page\n    frame = frames_[frame_id];\n    frame->Reset();\n\n    // Read the page from disk\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    // Update page table\n    page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n    // Set pin count and record access\n    frame->pin_count_.store(1);\n    replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n  }\n\n  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedWritePage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedWritePage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageWrite` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return WritePageGuard A page guard ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::WritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> WritePageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedWritePage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedWritePage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedReadPage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedReadPage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageRead` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return ReadPageGuard A page guard ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::ReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> ReadPageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedReadPage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedReadPage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk unsafely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should not take a lock on the page in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n  if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n  auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n  if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n    frame->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk safely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should take a lock on the page in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `Flush` in the page guards, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n    frame = frames_[frame_id];\n  }\n\n  // Take shared lock on the page to flush safely\n  std::shared_lock page_lock(frame->rwlatch_);\n\n  if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n    frame->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk unsafely.\n *\n * You should not take locks on the pages in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  for (auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {\n    auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];\n    if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n      frame->is_dirty_ = false;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk safely.\n *\n * You should take locks on the pages in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() {\n  // Get a copy of the page table first\n  std::vector<std::pair<page_id_t, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader>>> pages_to_flush;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    for (auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {\n      pages_to_flush.emplace_back(page_id, frames_[frame_id]);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Flush each page safely\n  for (auto &[page_id, frame] : pages_to_flush) {\n    std::shared_lock page_lock(frame->rwlatch_);\n    if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n      frame->is_dirty_ = false;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Retrieves the pin count of a page. If the page does not exist in memory, return `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * This function is thread safe. Callers may invoke this function in a multi-threaded environment where multiple threads\n * access the same page.\n *\n * This function is intended for testing purposes. If this function is implemented incorrectly, it will definitely cause\n * problems with the test suite and autograder.\n *\n * # Implementation\n *\n * We will use this function to test if your buffer pool manager is managing pin counts correctly. Since the\n * `pin_count_` field in `FrameHeader` is an atomic type, you do not need to take the latch on the frame that holds the\n * page we want to look at. Instead, you can simply use an atomic `load` to safely load the value stored. You will still\n * need to take the buffer pool latch, however.\n *\n * Again, if you are unfamiliar with atomic types, see the official C++ docs\n * [here](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic).\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to get the pin count of.\n * @return std::optional<size_t> The pin count if the page exists; otherwise, `std::nullopt`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n  if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n    return std::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n  return frames_[frame_id]->pin_count_.load();\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp",
  "new_string": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Check if page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      // Release BPM latch, then acquire frame lock in guard constructor\n      // to avoid deadlock\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Page not in buffer pool, need to find a frame\n      // First, try to get a free frame\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: No free frames, need to evict\n        auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted.has_value()) {\n          // Could not evict any frame\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted.value();\n\n        // Find the page currently in this frame\n        for (auto &[pid, fid] : page_table_) {\n          if (fid == frame_id) {\n            // If dirty, flush to disk\n            auto &evicted_frame = frames_[frame_id];\n            if (evicted_frame->is_dirty_) {\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, evicted_frame->GetDataMut(), pid, std::move(promise)});\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pid);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Now we have a frame, use it for the new page\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      requests.push_back(DiskRequest{false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Set pin count and record access\n      frame->pin_count_.store(1);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Guard constructor acquires frame lock AFTER BPM latch is released\n  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}",
  "old_string": "auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Check if page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      // Return the guard (lock will be acquired in constructor)\n      return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n    }\n\n    // Case 2: Page not in buffer pool, need to find a frame\n    // First, try to get a free frame\n    if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n      frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n      free_frames_.pop_front();\n    } else {\n      // Case 3: No free frames, need to evict\n      auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();\n      if (!evicted.has_value()) {\n        // Could not evict any frame\n        return std::nullopt;\n      }\n      frame_id = evicted.value();\n\n      // Find the page currently in this frame\n      for (auto &[pid, fid] : page_table_) {\n        if (fid == frame_id) {\n          // If dirty, flush to disk\n          auto &evicted_frame = frames_[frame_id];\n          if (evicted_frame->is_dirty_) {\n            auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n            auto future = promise.get_future();\n            std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n            requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, evicted_frame->GetDataMut(), pid, std::move(promise)});\n            disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n            future.get();\n          }\n          page_table_.erase(pid);\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Now we have a frame, use it for the new page\n    frame = frames_[frame_id];\n    frame->Reset();\n\n    // Read the page from disk\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    // Update page table\n    page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n    // Set pin count and record access\n    frame->pin_count_.store(1);\n    replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n  }\n\n  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}

it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n std::move(promise)});\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Set pin count and record access\n      frame->pin_count_.store(1);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Guard constructor {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Check if page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n disk_scheduler_);\n    }\n\n    // Case 2: Page not in buffer pool, need to find a frame\n    // First, try to get a free frame\n    if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n      frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n      free_frames_.pop_front();\n    } else {\n      // Case 3: No free frames, need to evict\n      auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();\n      if (!evicted.has_value()) {\n {\n        if (fid == frame_id) {\n          // If dirty, flush to disk\n          auto &evicted_frame = frames_[frame_id];\n          if (evicted_frame->is_dirty_) {\n            auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    // Update page table\n    page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n    // Set pin count and record access\n bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}",
  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\"\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The constructor for a `FrameHeader` that initializes all fields to default values.\n *\n * See the documentation for `FrameHeader` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * @param frame_id The frame ID / index of the frame we are creating a header for.\n */\nFrameHeader::FrameHeader(frame_id_t frame_id) : frame_id_(frame_id), data_(BUSTUB_PAGE_SIZE, 0) { Reset(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw const pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return const char* A pointer to immutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetData() const -> const char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Get a raw mutable pointer to the frame's data.\n *\n * @return char* A pointer to mutable data that the frame stores.\n */\nauto FrameHeader::GetDataMut() -> char * { return data_.data(); }\n\n/**\n * @brief Resets a `FrameHeader`'s member fields.\n */\nvoid FrameHeader::Reset() {\n  std::fill(data_.begin(), data_.end(), 0);\n  pin_count_.store(0);\n  is_dirty_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Creates a new `BufferPoolManager` instance and initializes all fields.\n *\n * See the documentation for `BufferPoolManager` in \"buffer/buffer_pool_manager.h\" for more information.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * We have implemented the constructor for you in a way that makes sense with our reference solution. You are free to\n * change anything you would like here if it doesn't fit with you implementation.\n *\n * Be warned, though! If you stray too far away from our guidance, it will be much harder for us to help you. Our\n * recommendation would be to first implement the buffer pool manager using the stepping stones we have provided.\n *\n * Once you have a fully working solution (all Gradescope test cases pass), then you can try more interesting things!\n *\n * @param num_frames The size of the buffer pool.\n * @param disk_manager The disk manager.\n * @param log_manager The log manager. Please ignore this for P1.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::BufferPoolManager(size_t num_frames, DiskManager *disk_manager, LogManager *log_manager)\n    : num_frames_(num_frames),\n      next_page_id_(0),\n      bpm_latch_(std::make_shared<std::mutex>()),\n      replacer_(std::make_shared<ArcReplacer>(num_frames)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::make_shared<DiskScheduler>(disk_manager)),\n      log_manager_(log_manager) {\n  // Not strictly necessary...\n  std::scoped_lock latch(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Initialize the monotonically increasing counter at 0.\n  next_page_id_.store(0);\n\n  // Allocate all of the in-memory frames up front.\n  frames_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // The page table should have exactly `num_frames_` slots, corresponding to exactly `num_frames_` frames.\n  page_table_.reserve(num_frames_);\n\n  // Initialize all of the frame headers, and fill the free frame list with all possible frame IDs (since all frames are\n  // initially free).\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < num_frames_; i++) {\n    frames_.push_back(std::make_shared<FrameHeader>(i));\n    free_frames_.push_back(static_cast<int>(i));\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Destroys the `BufferPoolManager`, freeing up all memory that the buffer pool was using.\n */\nBufferPoolManager::~BufferPoolManager() = default;\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns the number of frames that this buffer pool manages.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::Size() const -> size_t { return num_frames_; }\n\n/**\n * @brief Allocates a new page on disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You will maintain a thread-safe, monotonically increasing counter in the form of a `std::atomic<page_id_t>`.\n * See the documentation on [atomics](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic) for more information.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @return The page ID of the newly allocated page.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t {\n  // Atomically increment and return the page id\n  return next_page_id_.fetch_add(1);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Removes a page from the database, both on disk and in memory.\n *\n * If the page is pinned in the buffer pool, this function does nothing and returns `false`. Otherwise, this function\n * removes the page from both disk and memory (if it is still in the buffer pool), returning `true`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Think about all of the places that a page or a page's metadata could be, and use that to guide you on implementing\n * this function. You will probably want to implement this function _after_ you have implemented `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * You should call `DeallocatePage` in the disk scheduler to make the space available for new pages.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to delete.\n * @return `false` if the page exists but could not be deleted, `true` if the page didn't exist or deletion succeeded.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  // Check if page is in memory\n  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n  if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n    // Page not in memory, just deallocate from disk\n    disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n  auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n  // Check if the page is pinned\n  if (frame->pin_count_.load() > 0) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  // Remove from page table\n  page_table_.erase(it);\n\n  // Remove from replacer\n  replacer_->Remove(frame_id);\n\n  // Reset the frame\n  frame->Reset();\n\n  // Add frame back to free list\n  free_frames_.push_back(frame_id);\n\n  // Deallocate page from disk\n  disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional write-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can only be 1 `WritePageGuard` reading/writing a page at a time. This allows data access to be both immutable\n * and mutable, meaning the thread that owns the `WritePageGuard` is allowed to manipulate the page's data however they\n * want. If a user wants to have multiple threads reading the page at the same time, they must acquire a `ReadPageGuard`\n * with `CheckedReadPage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * There are three main cases that you will have to implement. The first two are relatively simple: one is when there is\n * plenty of available memory, and the other is when we don't actually need to perform any additional I/O. Think about\n * what exactly these two cases entail.\n *\n * The third case is the trickiest, and it is when we do not have any _easily_ available memory at our disposal. The\n * buffer pool is tasked with finding memory that it can use to bring in a page of memory, using the replacement\n * algorithm you implemented previously to find candidate frames for eviction.\n *\n * Once the buffer pool has identified a frame for eviction, several I/O operations may be necessary to bring in the\n * page of data we want into the frame.\n *\n * There is likely going to be a lot of shared code with `CheckedReadPage`, so you may find creating helper functions\n * useful.\n *\n * These two functions are the crux of this project, so we won't give you more hints than this. Good luck!\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<WritePageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `WritePageGuard` ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Check if page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      // Release BPM latch, then acquire frame lock in guard constructor\n      // to avoid deadlock\n    } else {\n      // Case 2: Page not in buffer pool, need to find a frame\n      // First, try to get a free frame\n      if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n        frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n        free_frames_.pop_front();\n      } else {\n        // Case 3: No free frames, need to evict\n        auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();\n        if (!evicted.has_value()) {\n          // Could not evict any frame\n          return std::nullopt;\n        }\n        frame_id = evicted.value();\n\n        // Find the page currently in this frame\n        for (auto &[pid, fid] : page_table_) {\n          if (fid == frame_id) {\n            // If dirty, flush to disk\n            auto &evicted_frame = frames_[frame_id];\n            if (evicted_frame->is_dirty_) {\n              auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n              auto future = promise.get_future();\n              std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n              requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, evicted_frame->GetDataMut(), pid, std::move(promise)});\n              disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n              future.get();\n            }\n            page_table_.erase(pid);\n            break;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Now we have a frame, use it for the new page\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      frame->Reset();\n\n      // Read the page from disk\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      requests.push_back(DiskRequest{false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n\n      // Update page table\n      page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n      // Set pin count and record access\n      frame->pin_count_.store(1);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Guard constructor acquires frame lock AFTER BPM latch is released\n  return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Acquires an optional read-locked guard over a page of data. The user can specify an `AccessType` if needed.\n *\n * If it is not possible to bring the page of data into memory, this function will return a `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * Page data can _only_ be accessed via page guards. Users of this `BufferPoolManager` are expected to acquire either a\n * `ReadPageGuard` or a `WritePageGuard` depending on the mode in which they would like to access the data, which\n * ensures that any access of data is thread-safe.\n *\n * There can be any number of `ReadPageGuard`s reading the same page of data at a time across different threads.\n * However, all data access must be immutable. If a user wants to mutate the page's data, they must acquire a\n * `WritePageGuard` with `CheckedWritePage` instead.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * See the implementation details of `CheckedWritePage`.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return std::optional<ReadPageGuard> An optional latch guard where if there are no more free frames (out of memory)\n * returns `std::nullopt`; otherwise, returns a `ReadPageGuard` ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::CheckedReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<ReadPageGuard> {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n  frame_id_t frame_id;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    // Case 1: Check if page is already in the buffer pool\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it != page_table_.end()) {\n      frame_id = it->second;\n      frame = frames_[frame_id];\n      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable\n      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n      // Return the guard (lock will be acquired in constructor)\n      return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n    }\n\n    // Case 2: Page not in buffer pool, need to find a frame\n    // First, try to get a free frame\n    if (!free_frames_.empty()) {\n      frame_id = free_frames_.front();\n      free_frames_.pop_front();\n    } else {\n      // Case 3: No free frames, need to evict\n      auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();\n      if (!evicted.has_value()) {\n        // Could not evict any frame\n        return std::nullopt;\n      }\n      frame_id = evicted.value();\n\n      // Find the page currently in this frame\n      for (auto &[pid, fid] : page_table_) {\n        if (fid == frame_id) {\n          // If dirty, flush to disk\n          auto &evicted_frame = frames_[frame_id];\n          if (evicted_frame->is_dirty_) {\n            auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n            auto future = promise.get_future();\n            std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n            requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, evicted_frame->GetDataMut(), pid, std::move(promise)});\n            disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n            future.get();\n          }\n          page_table_.erase(pid);\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Now we have a frame, use it for the new page\n    frame = frames_[frame_id];\n    frame->Reset();\n\n    // Read the page from disk\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{false, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n\n    // Update page table\n    page_table_[page_id] = frame_id;\n\n    // Set pin count and record access\n    frame->pin_count_.store(1);\n    replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);\n  }\n\n  return ReadPageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedWritePage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedWritePage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageWrite` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return WritePageGuard A page guard ensuring exclusive and mutable access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::WritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> WritePageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedWritePage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedWritePage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief A wrapper around `CheckedReadPage` that unwraps the inner value if it exists.\n *\n * If `CheckedReadPage` returns a `std::nullopt`, **this function aborts the entire process.**\n *\n * This function should **only** be used for testing and ergonomic's sake. If it is at all possible that the buffer pool\n * manager might run out of memory, then use `CheckedPageWrite` to allow you to handle that case.\n *\n * See the documentation for `CheckedPageRead` for more information about implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param access_type The type of page access.\n * @return ReadPageGuard A page guard ensuring shared and read-only access to a page's data.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::ReadPage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> ReadPageGuard {\n  auto guard_opt = CheckedReadPage(page_id, access_type);\n\n  if (!guard_opt.has_value()) {\n    fmt::println(stderr, \"\\n`CheckedReadPage` failed to bring in page {}\\n\", page_id);\n    std::abort();\n  }\n\n  return std::move(guard_opt).value();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk unsafely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should not take a lock on the page in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage` and\n * `CheckedWritePage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n  if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n  auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];\n\n  if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n    frame->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes a page's data out to disk safely.\n *\n * This function will write out a page's data to disk if it has been modified. If the given page is not in memory, this\n * function will return `false`.\n *\n * You should take a lock on the page in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `Flush` in the page guards, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page to be flushed.\n * @return `false` if the page could not be found in the page table; otherwise, `true`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {\n  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n    if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n    frame = frames_[frame_id];\n  }\n\n  // Take shared lock on the page to flush safely\n  std::shared_lock page_lock(frame->rwlatch_);\n\n  if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n    future.get();\n    frame->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk unsafely.\n *\n * You should not take locks on the pages in this function.\n * This means that you should carefully consider when to toggle the `is_dirty_` bit.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  for (auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {\n    auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];\n    if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n      frame->is_dirty_ = false;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes all page data that is in memory to disk safely.\n *\n * You should take locks on the pages in this function to ensure that a consistent state is flushed to disk.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * You should probably leave implementing this function until after you have completed `CheckedReadPage`,\n * `CheckedWritePage`, and `FlushPage`, as it will likely be much easier to understand what to do.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n */\nvoid BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() {\n  // Get a copy of the page table first\n  std::vector<std::pair<page_id_t, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader>>> pages_to_flush;\n\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    for (auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {\n      pages_to_flush.emplace_back(page_id, frames_[frame_id]);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Flush each page safely\n  for (auto &[page_id, frame] : pages_to_flush) {\n    std::shared_lock page_lock(frame->rwlatch_);\n    if (frame->is_dirty_) {\n      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n      auto future = promise.get_future();\n      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n      requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});\n      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n      future.get();\n      frame->is_dirty_ = false;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Retrieves the pin count of a page. If the page does not exist in memory, return `std::nullopt`.\n *\n * This function is thread safe. Callers may invoke this function in a multi-threaded environment where multiple threads\n * access the same page.\n *\n * This function is intended for testing purposes. If this function is implemented incorrectly, it will definitely cause\n * problems with the test suite and autograder.\n *\n * # Implementation\n *\n * We will use this function to test if your buffer pool manager is managing pin counts correctly. Since the\n * `pin_count_` field in `FrameHeader` is an atomic type, you do not need to take the latch on the frame that holds the\n * page we want to look at. Instead, you can simply use an atomic `load` to safely load the value stored. You will still\n * need to take the buffer pool latch, however.\n *\n * Again, if you are unfamiliar with atomic types, see the official C++ docs\n * [here](https://en.cppreference.com/w/cpp/atomic/atomic).\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to get the pin count of.\n * @return std::optional<size_t> The pin count if the page exists; otherwise, `std::nullopt`.\n */\nauto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n\n  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);\n  if (it == page_table_.end()) {\n    return std::nullopt;\n  }\n\n  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;\n  return frames_[frame_id]->pin_count_.load();\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",

  "file": {
    "content": " * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list\n  auto evict_from_list = [this](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,\n                                std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {\n      frame_id_t frame_id = *it;\n      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {\n        // Found an evictable frame\n        page_id_t page_id = status_it->second->page_id_;\n        ArcStatus status = status_it->second->arc_status_;\n\n        // Remove from alive list\n        list.erase(it);\n        alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n        curr_size_--;\n\n        // Add to corresponding ghost list\n        auto ghost_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(\n            page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false,\n            (status == ArcStatus::MRU) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST);\n        ghost_list.push_back(page_id);\n        ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status;\n\n        // Limit ghost list size to ensure total size <= 2 * replacer_size_\n        while (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mru_ghost_.front();\n            mru_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n        while (mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();\n            mfu_ghost_.pop_front();",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
    "numLines": 40,
    "startLine": 45,
    "totalLines": 334
  },
  "type": "text"

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp",
  "newString": "  // Determine which list to evict from based on target size\n  // If mru_.size() >= mru_target_size_, evict from mru\n  // Otherwise evict from mfu\n  size_t mru_alive_size = mru_.size();\n\n  if (mru_alive_size >= mru_target_size_) {\n    // Try to evict from mru first\n    auto result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n    if (result.has_value()) {\n      return result;\n    }\n    // If mru has no evictable frames, try mfu\n    return evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  }\n  // Try to evict from mfu first\n  auto result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  if (result.has_value()) {\n    return result;\n  }\n  // If mfu has no evictable frames, try mru",
  "oldString": "  // Determine which list to evict from based on target size\n  // If mru_.size() > mru_target_size_, evict from mru\n  // Otherwise evict from mfu\n  size_t mru_alive_size = mru_.size();\n\n  if (mru_alive_size > mru_target_size_) {\n    // Try to evict from mru first\n    auto result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n    if (result.has_value()) {\n      return result;\n    }\n    // If mru has no evictable frames, try mfu\n    return evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  }\n  // Try to evict from mfu first\n  auto result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  if (result.has_value()) {\n    return result;\n  }\n  // If mfu has no evictable frames, try mru",
  "originalFile": "// :bustub-keep-private:\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include <optional>\n#include \"common/config.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief a new ArcReplacer, with lists initialized to be empty and target size to 0\n * @param num_frames the maximum number of frames the ArcReplacer will be required to cache\n */\nArcReplacer::ArcReplacer(size_t num_frames) : replacer_size_(num_frames) {}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup\n * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ into its corresponding ghost list\n * according to balancing policy.\n *\n * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are\n * two changes in our implementation:\n * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check\n * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.\n * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.\n * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side\n * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),\n * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).\n *\n * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list\n  auto evict_from_list = [this](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,\n                                std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {\n    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {\n      frame_id_t frame_id = *it;\n      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {\n        // Found an evictable frame\n        page_id_t page_id = status_it->second->page_id_;\n        ArcStatus status = status_it->second->arc_status_;\n\n        // Remove from alive list\n        list.erase(it);\n        alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n        curr_size_--;\n\n        // Add to corresponding ghost list\n        auto ghost_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(\n            page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false,\n            (status == ArcStatus::MRU) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST);\n        ghost_list.push_back(page_id);\n        ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status;\n\n        // Limit ghost list size to ensure total size <= 2 * replacer_size_\n        while (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mru_ghost_.front();\n            mru_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n        while (mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {\n          if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n            page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();\n            mfu_ghost_.pop_front();\n            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n          }\n        }\n\n        return frame_id;\n      }\n    }\n    return std::nullopt;\n  };\n\n  // Determine which list to evict from based on target size\n  // If mru_.size() > mru_target_size_, evict from mru\n  // Otherwise evict from mfu\n  size_t mru_alive_size = mru_.size();\n\n  if (mru_alive_size > mru_target_size_) {\n    // Try to evict from mru first\n    auto result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n    if (result.has_value()) {\n      return result;\n    }\n    // If mru has no evictable frames, try mfu\n    return evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  }\n  // Try to evict from mfu first\n  auto result = evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);\n  if (result.has_value()) {\n    return result;\n  }\n  // If mfu has no evictable frames, try mru\n  return evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Record access to a frame, adjusting ARC bookkeeping accordingly\n * by bring the accessed page to the front of mfu_ if it exists in any of the lists\n * or the front of mru_ if it does not.\n *\n * Performs the operations EXCEPT REPLACE described in original paper, which is\n * handled by `Evict()`.\n *\n * Consider the following four cases, handle accordingly:\n * 1. Access hits mru_ or mfu_\n * 2/3. Access hits mru_ghost_ / mfu_ghost_\n * 4. Access misses all the lists\n *\n * This routine performs all changes to the four lists as preperation\n * for `Evict()` to simply find and evict a victim into ghost lists.\n *\n * Note that frame_id is used as identifier for alive pages and\n * page_id is used as identifier for the ghost pages, since page_id is\n * the unique identifier to the page after it's dead.\n * Using page_id for alive pages should be the same since it's one to one mapping,\n * but using frame_id is slightly more intuitive.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame that received a new access.\n * @param page_id id of page that is mapped to the frame.\n * @param access_type type of access that was received. This parameter is only needed for\n * leaderboard tests.\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  // Case 1: Access hits mru_ or mfu_ (frame is already in alive_map)\n  auto alive_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n  if (alive_it != alive_map_.end()) {\n    auto &status = alive_it->second;\n    // Move to mfu (frequent use)\n    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n      // Remove from mru list\n      mru_.remove(frame_id);\n      // Add to back of mfu list\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n      status->arc_status_ = ArcStatus::MFU;\n    } else {\n      // Already in mfu, move to back (most recently used)\n      mfu_.remove(frame_id);\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Case 2: Access hits mru_ghost_\n  auto ghost_it = ghost_map_.find(page_id);\n  if (ghost_it != ghost_map_.end()) {\n    auto &ghost_status = ghost_it->second;\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST) {\n      // Increase target size: delta = max(1, |mfu_ghost| / |mru_ghost|)\n      size_t delta = std::max(static_cast<size_t>(1), mfu_ghost_.size() / std::max(mru_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)));\n      mru_target_size_ = std::min(mru_target_size_ + delta, replacer_size_);\n\n      // Remove from ghost list\n      mru_ghost_.remove(page_id);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to mfu list\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n    // Case 3: Access hits mfu_ghost_\n    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST) {\n      // Decrease target size: delta = max(1, |mru_ghost| / |mfu_ghost|)\n      size_t delta = std::max(static_cast<size_t>(1), mru_ghost_.size() / std::max(mfu_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)));\n      if (mru_target_size_ >= delta) {\n        mru_target_size_ -= delta;\n      } else {\n        mru_target_size_ = 0;\n      }\n\n      // Remove from ghost list\n      mfu_ghost_.remove(page_id);\n      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);\n\n      // Add to mfu list\n      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);\n      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);\n      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n      return;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Case 4: Access misses all the lists - new page\n  // Case 4A: mru_ + mru_ghost_ >= replacer_size_\n  if (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() >= replacer_size_) {\n    if (mru_.size() < replacer_size_) {\n      // Remove oldest from mru_ghost_\n      if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {\n        page_id_t old_page = mru_ghost_.front();\n        mru_ghost_.pop_front();\n        ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n      }\n    }\n  } else {\n    // Case 4B: mru_ + mru_ghost_ < replacer_size_\n    // Total size might equal 2 * replacer_size_, need to remove from mfu_ghost_\n    size_t total_size = mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() + mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size();\n    if (total_size >= 2 * replacer_size_) {\n      if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {\n        page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();\n        mfu_ghost_.pop_front();\n        ghost_map_.erase(old_page);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Insert new page into mru list\n  auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MRU);\n  mru_.push_back(frame_id);\n  alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Toggle whether a frame is evictable or non-evictable. This function also\n * controls replacer's size. Note that size is equal to number of evictable entries.\n *\n * If a frame was previously evictable and is to be set to non-evictable, then size should\n * decrement. If a frame was previously non-evictable and is to be set to evictable,\n * then size should increment.\n *\n * If frame id is invalid, throw an exception or abort the process.\n *\n * For other scenarios, this function should terminate without modifying anything.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame whose 'evictable' status will be modified\n * @param set_evictable whether the given frame is evictable or not\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  auto it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n  if (it == alive_map_.end()) {\n    // Frame not found - this is okay per the spec (just return)\n    return;\n  }\n\n  auto &status = it->second;\n  if (status->evictable_ && !set_evictable) {\n    // Was evictable, now non-evictable\n    status->evictable_ = false;\n    curr_size_--;\n  } else if (!status->evictable_ && set_evictable) {\n    // Was non-evictable, now evictable\n    status->evictable_ = true;\n    curr_size_++;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Remove an evictable frame from replacer.\n * This function should also decrement replacer's size if removal is successful.\n *\n * Note that this is different from evicting a frame, which always remove the frame\n * decided by the ARC algorithm.\n *\n * If Remove is called on a non-evictable frame, throw an exception or abort the\n * process.\n *\n * If specified frame is not found, directly return from this function.\n *\n * @param frame_id id of frame to be removed\n */\nvoid ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n\n  auto it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);\n  if (it == alive_map_.end()) {\n    // Frame not found, just return\n    return;\n  }\n\n  auto &status = it->second;\n  if (!status->evictable_) {\n    // Cannot remove a non-evictable frame\n    throw std::runtime_error(\"Cannot remove a non-evictable frame\");\n  }\n\n  // Remove from the appropriate list\n  if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {\n    mru_.remove(frame_id);\n  } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {\n    mfu_.remove(frame_id);\n  }\n\n  // Remove from alive_map and decrement size\n  alive_map_.erase(frame_id);\n  curr_size_--;\n}\n\n/**\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation\n *\n * @brief Return replacer's size, which tracks the number of evictable frames.\n *\n * @return size_t\n */\nauto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t {\n  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);\n  return curr_size_;\n}\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/buffer_pool_manager_test.cpp",
  "limit": 70,
  "offset": 360
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "TEST(BufferPoolManagerTest, DISABLED_EvictableTest) {\n  // Test if the evictable status of a frame is always correct.\n  const size_t rounds = 1000;\n  const size_t num_readers = 8;\n\n  auto disk_manager = std::make_shared<DiskManager>(db_fname);\n  // Only allocate one frame of memory to the buffer pool manager.\n  auto bpm = std::make_shared<BufferPoolManager>(1, disk_manager.get());\n\n  for (size_t i = 0; i < rounds; i++) {\n    std::mutex mutex;\n    std::condition_variable cv;\n\n    // This signal tells the readers that they can start reading after the main thread has already taken the read latch.\n    bool signal = false;\n\n    // This page will be loaded into the only available frame.\n    const auto winner_pid = bpm->NewPage();\n    // We will attempt to load this page into the occupied frame, and it should fail every time.\n    const auto loser_pid = bpm->NewPage();\n\n    std::vector<std::thread> readers;\n    for (size_t j = 0; j < num_readers; j++) {\n      readers.emplace_back([&]() {\n        std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex);\n\n        // Wait until the main thread has taken a read latch on the page.\n        while (!signal) {\n          cv.wait(lock);\n        }\n\n        // Read the page in shared mode.\n        const auto read_guard = bpm->ReadPage(winner_pid);\n\n        // Since the only frame is pinned, no thread should be able to bring in a new page.\n        ASSERT_FALSE(bpm->CheckedReadPage(loser_pid).has_value());\n      });\n    }\n\n    std::unique_lock<std::mutex> lock(mutex);\n\n    if (i % 2 == 0) {\n      // Take the read latch on the page and pin it.\n      auto read_guard = bpm->ReadPage(winner_pid);\n\n      // Wake up all of the readers.\n      signal = true;\n      cv.notify_all();\n      lock.unlock();\n\n      // Allow other threads to read.\n      read_guard.Drop();\n    } else {\n      // Take the read latch on the page and pin it.\n      auto write_guard = bpm->WritePage(winner_pid);\n\n      // Wake up all of the readers.\n      signal = true;\n      cv.notify_all();\n      lock.unlock();\n\n      // Allow other threads to read.\n      write_guard.Drop();\n    }\n\n    for (size_t i = 0; i < num_readers; i++) {\n      readers[i].join();\n    }\n  }\n}",
    "filePath": "/workspace/bustub/test/buffer/buffer_pool_manager_test.cpp",
    "numLines": 70,
    "startLine": 360,
    "totalLines": 432
  },
  "type": "text"
}
