  "file": {
    "content": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // Acquire shared lock for reading\n  frame_->rwlatch_.lock_shared();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the other guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // Release resources held by this object first\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move data from that to this\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the other guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Create a write request\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame_->GetDataMut(), page_id_, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n\n    // Wait for the write to complete\n    future.get();\n\n    // Clear dirty flag\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Release the shared lock first\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count\n  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;\n\n  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable\n  if (pin_count == 0) {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Mark this guard as invalid\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nReadPageGuard::~ReadPageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // Acquire exclusive lock for writing\n  frame_->rwlatch_.lock();\n  // Mark the page as dirty since we're getting write access\n  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the other guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return WritePageGuard& The newly valid `WritePageGuard`.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // Release resources held by this object first\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move data from that to this\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the other guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetDataMut();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Create a write request\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame_->GetDataMut(), page_id_, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n\n    // Wait for the write to complete\n    future.get();\n\n    // Clear dirty flag\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `WritePageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Release the exclusive lock first\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();\n\n  // Decrement pin count\n  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;\n\n  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable\n  if (pin_count == 0) {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Mark this guard as invalid\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `WritePageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nWritePageGuard::~WritePageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n}  // namespace bustub\n",

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp",
  "new_string": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Release the shared lock first\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Take BPM latch and decrement pin count atomically with evictable check\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;\n    // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable\n    if (pin_count == 0) {\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Mark this guard as invalid\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "old_string": "void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Release the shared lock first\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Decrement pin count\n  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;\n\n  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable\n  if (pin_count == 0) {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Mark this guard as invalid\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "replace_all": false
}

  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // Acquire shared lock for reading\n  frame_->rwlatch_.lock_shared();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the other guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // Release resources held by this object first\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move data from that to this\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the other guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetDataMut();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Create a write request\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame_->GetDataMut(), page_id_, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n\n    // Wait for the write to complete\n    future.get();\n\n    // Clear dirty flag\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `WritePageGuard`'s data. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid WritePageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Release the exclusive lock first\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();\n\n  // Decrement pin count\n  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;\n\n  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable\n  if (pin_count == 0) {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Mark this guard as invalid\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `WritePageGuard`.   "newString": "void WritePageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Release the exclusive lock first\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();\n\n  // Take BPM latch and decrement pin count atomically with evictable check\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;\n    // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable\n    if (pin_count == 0) {\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Mark this guard as invalid\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "oldString": "void WritePageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Release the exclusive lock first\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();\n\n  // Decrement pin count\n  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;\n\n  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable\n  if (pin_count == 0) {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n  }\n\n  // Mark this guard as invalid\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}",
  "originalFile": "//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n//\n//                         BusTub\n//\n// page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Identification: src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp\n//\n// Copyright (c) 2015-2025, Carnegie Mellon University Database Group\n//\n//===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n\n#include \"storage/page/page_guard.h\"\n#include <memory>\n#include \"buffer/arc_replacer.h\"\n#include \"common/macros.h\"\n\nnamespace bustub {\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `ReadPageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to read.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // Acquire shared lock for reading\n  frame_->rwlatch_.lock_shared();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the other guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `ReadPageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return ReadPageGuard& The newly valid `ReadPageGuard`.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // Release resources held by this object first\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move data from that to this\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the other guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Returns whether the page is dirty (modified but not flushed to the disk).\n */\nauto ReadPageGuard::IsDirty() const -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Flush() {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid read guard\");\n\n  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {\n    // Create a write request\n    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();\n    auto future = promise.get_future();\n\n    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;\n    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame_->GetDataMut(), page_id_, std::move(promise)});\n    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);\n\n    // Wait for the write to complete\n    future.get();\n\n    // Clear dirty flag\n    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Manually drops a valid `ReadPageGuard`'s data. If this guard is invalid, this function does nothing.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * Make sure you don't double free! Also, think **very** **VERY** carefully about what resources you own and the order\n * in which you release those resources. If you get the ordering wrong, you will very likely fail one of the later\n * Gradescope tests. You may also want to take the buffer pool manager's latch in a very specific scenario...\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n */\nvoid ReadPageGuard::Drop() {\n  if (!is_valid_) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Release the shared lock first\n  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();\n\n  // Take BPM latch and decrement pin count atomically with evictable check\n  {\n    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);\n    size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;\n    // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable\n    if (pin_count == 0) {\n      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Mark this guard as invalid\n  is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/** @brief The destructor for `ReadPageGuard`. This destructor simply calls `Drop()`. */\nReadPageGuard::~ReadPageGuard() { Drop(); }\n\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n/**********************************************************************************************************************/\n\n/**\n * @brief The only constructor for an RAII `WritePageGuard` that creates a valid guard.\n *\n * Note that only the buffer pool manager is allowed to call this constructor.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param page_id The page ID of the page we want to write to.\n * @param frame A shared pointer to the frame that holds the page we want to protect.\n * @param replacer A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's replacer.\n * @param bpm_latch A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's latch.\n * @param disk_scheduler A shared pointer to the buffer pool manager's disk scheduler.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,\n                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,\n                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)\n    : page_id_(page_id),\n      frame_(std::move(frame)),\n      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),\n      is_valid_(true) {\n  // Acquire exclusive lock for writing\n  frame_->rwlatch_.lock();\n  // Mark the page as dirty since we're getting write access\n  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move constructor for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n */\nWritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept\n    : page_id_(that.page_id_),\n      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),\n      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),\n      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),\n      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),\n      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {\n  // Invalidate the other guard\n  that.is_valid_ = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief The move assignment operator for `WritePageGuard`.\n *\n * ### Implementation\n *\n * If you are unfamiliar with move semantics, please familiarize yourself with learning materials online. There are many\n * great resources (including articles, Microsoft tutorials, YouTube videos) that explain this in depth.\n *\n * Make sure you invalidate the other guard; otherwise, you might run into double free problems! For both objects, you\n * need to update _at least_ 5 fields each, and for the current object, make sure you release any resources it might be\n * holding on to.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add implementation.\n *\n * @param that The other page guard.\n * @return WritePageGuard& The newly valid `WritePageGuard`.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & {\n  if (this != &that) {\n    // Release resources held by this object first\n    Drop();\n\n    // Move data from that to this\n    page_id_ = that.page_id_;\n    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);\n    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);\n    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);\n    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);\n    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;\n\n    // Invalidate the other guard\n    that.is_valid_ = false;\n  }\n  return *this;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets the page ID of the page this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetPageId() const -> page_id_t {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return page_id_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a `const` pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetData() const -> const char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->GetData();\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Gets a mutable pointer to the page of data this guard is protecting.\n */\nauto WritePageGuard::GetDataMut() -> char * {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to -> bool {\n  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, \"tried to use an invalid write guard\");\n  return frame_->is_dirty_;\n}\n\n/**\n * @brief Flushes this page's data safely to disk.\n *\n * TODO(P1): Add 1. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
/**
 * TODO(P1): Add implementation
 *
 * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup
 * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ according to balancing policy.
 *
 * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are
 * two changes in our implementation:
 * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check
 * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.
 * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.
 * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side
 * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),
 * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).
 *
 * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict
 */
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }
After
/**
 * TODO(P1): Add implementation
 *
 * @brief Performs the Replace operation as described by the writeup
 * that evicts from either mfu_ or mru_ according to balancing policy.
 *
 * If you wish to refer to the original ARC paper, please note that there are
 * two changes in our implementation:
 * 1. When the size of mru_ equals the target size, we don't check
 * the last access as the paper did when deciding which list to evict from.
 * This is fine since the original decision is stated to be arbitrary.
 * 2. Entries that are not evictable are skipped. If all entries from the desired side
 * (mru_ / mfu_) are pinned, we instead try victimize the other side (mfu_ / mru_),
 * and move it to its corresponding ghost list (mfu_ghost_ / mru_ghost_).
 *
 * @return frame id of the evicted frame, or std::nullopt if cannot evict
 */
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list
  auto evict_from_list = [this](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,
                                std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {
      frame_id_t frame_id = *it;
      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);
      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {
        // Found an evictable frame
        page_id_t page_id = status_it->second->page_id_;
        ArcStatus status = status_it->second->arc_status_;

        // Remove from alive list
        list.erase(it);
        alive_map_.erase(frame_id);
        curr_size_--;

        // Add to corresponding ghost list
        auto ghost_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(
            page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false,
            (status == ArcSt
... (truncated)
2. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> { return std::nullopt; }
After
auto ArcReplacer::Evict() -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  // Helper lambda to find and evict from a list
  auto evict_from_list = [this](std::list<frame_id_t> &list,
                                std::list<page_id_t> &ghost_list) -> std::optional<frame_id_t> {
    for (auto it = list.begin(); it != list.end(); ++it) {
      frame_id_t frame_id = *it;
      auto status_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);
      if (status_it != alive_map_.end() && status_it->second->evictable_) {
        // Found an evictable frame
        page_id_t page_id = status_it->second->page_id_;
        ArcStatus status = status_it->second->arc_status_;

        // Remove from alive list
        list.erase(it);
        alive_map_.erase(frame_id);
        curr_size_--;

        // Add to corresponding ghost list
        auto ghost_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(
            page_id, INVALID_FRAME_ID, false,
            (status == ArcStatus::MRU) ? ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST : ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST);
        ghost_list.push_back(page_id);
        ghost_map_[page_id] = ghost_status;

        // Limit ghost list size to ensure total size <= 2 * replacer_size_
        while (mru_.size() + mru_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {
          if (!mru_ghost_.empty()) {
            page_id_t old_page = mru_ghost_.front();
            mru_ghost_.pop_front();
            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);
          }
        }
        while (mfu_.size() + mfu_ghost_.size() > replacer_size_) {
          if (!mfu_ghost_.empty()) {
            page_id_t old_page = mfu_ghost_.front();
            mfu_ghost_.pop_front();
            ghost_map_.erase(old_page);
          }
        }

        return frame_id;
      }
    }
    return std::nullopt;
  };

  // Determine which list to evict fro
... (truncated)
3. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::RecordAccess(frame_id_t frame_id, page_id_t page_id, [[maybe_unused]] AccessType access_type) {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  // Case 1: Access hits mru_ or mfu_ (frame is already in alive_map)
  auto alive_it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);
  if (alive_it != alive_map_.end()) {
    auto &status = alive_it->second;
    // Move to mfu (frequent use)
    if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {
      // Remove from mru list
      mru_.remove(frame_id);
      // Add to back of mfu list
      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);
      status->arc_status_ = ArcStatus::MFU;
    } else {
      // Already in mfu, move to back (most recently used)
      mfu_.remove(frame_id);
      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);
    }
    return;
  }

  // Case 2: Access hits mru_ghost_
  auto ghost_it = ghost_map_.find(page_id);
  if (ghost_it != ghost_map_.end()) {
    auto &ghost_status = ghost_it->second;
    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU_GHOST) {
      // Increase target size: delta = max(1, |mfu_ghost| / |mru_ghost|)
      size_t delta = std::max(static_cast<size_t>(1), mfu_ghost_.size() / std::max(mru_ghost_.size(), static_cast<size_t>(1)));
      mru_target_size_ = std::min(mru_target_size_ + delta, replacer_size_);

      // Remove from ghost list
      mru_ghost_.remove(page_id);
      ghost_map_.erase(page_id);

      // Add to mfu list
      auto new_status = std::make_shared<FrameStatus>(page_id, frame_id, false, ArcStatus::MFU);
      mfu_.push_back(frame_id);
      alive_map_[frame_id] = new_status;
      return;
    }
    // Case 3: Access hits mfu_ghost_
    if (ghost_status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU_GHOST) {
      // Decrease target size: delta = max(1, |mru_ghost| / |mfu_ghost|)
      size_t delta = std::max(static_cast<size_t>(1), mru_ghost_.si
... (truncated)
4. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::SetEvictable(frame_id_t frame_id, bool set_evictable) {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  auto it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);
  if (it == alive_map_.end()) {
    // Frame not found - this is okay per the spec (just return)
    return;
  }

  auto &status = it->second;
  if (status->evictable_ && !set_evictable) {
    // Was evictable, now non-evictable
    status->evictable_ = false;
    curr_size_--;
  } else if (!status->evictable_ && set_evictable) {
    // Was non-evictable, now evictable
    status->evictable_ = true;
    curr_size_++;
  }
}
5. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
void ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {}
After
void ArcReplacer::Remove(frame_id_t frame_id) {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);

  auto it = alive_map_.find(frame_id);
  if (it == alive_map_.end()) {
    // Frame not found, just return
    return;
  }

  auto &status = it->second;
  if (!status->evictable_) {
    // Cannot remove a non-evictable frame
    throw std::runtime_error("Cannot remove a non-evictable frame");
  }

  // Remove from the appropriate list
  if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MRU) {
    mru_.remove(frame_id);
  } else if (status->arc_status_ == ArcStatus::MFU) {
    mfu_.remove(frame_id);
  }

  // Remove from alive_map and decrement size
  alive_map_.erase(frame_id);
  curr_size_--;
}
6. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/arc_replacer.cpp
Before
auto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t { return 0; }
After
auto ArcReplacer::Size() -> size_t {
  std::scoped_lock lock(latch_);
  return curr_size_;
}

Before
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                             std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                             std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),
      is_valid_(true) {
  // Acquire shared lock for reading
  frame_->rwlatch_.lock_shared();
}
8. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept {}
After
ReadPageGuard::ReadPageGuard(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept
    : page_id_(that.page_id_),
      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),
      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),
      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {
  // Invalidate the other guard
  that.is_valid_ = false;
}
9. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
auto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & { return *this; }
After
auto ReadPageGuard::operator=(ReadPageGuard &&that) noexcept -> ReadPageGuard & {
  if (this != &that) {
    // Release resources held by this object first
    Drop();

    // Move data from that to this
    page_id_ = that.page_id_;
    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);
    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);
    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);
    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);
    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;

    // Invalidate the other guard
    that.is_valid_ = false;
  }
  return *this;
}
10. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void ReadPageGuard::Flush() {
  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, "tried to use an invalid read guard");

  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {
    // Create a write request
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();

    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame_->GetDataMut(), page_id_, std::move(promise)});
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);

    // Wait for the write to complete
    future.get();

    // Clear dirty flag
    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;
  }
}
11. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Release the shared lock first
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Decrement pin count
  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;

  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable
  if (pin_count == 0) {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Mark this guard as invalid
  is_valid_ = false;
}
12. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)) {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(page_id_t page_id, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame,
                               std::shared_ptr<ArcReplacer> replacer, std::shared_ptr<std::mutex> bpm_latch,
                               std::shared_ptr<DiskScheduler> disk_scheduler)
    : page_id_(page_id),
      frame_(std::move(frame)),
      replacer_(std::move(replacer)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(bpm_latch)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(disk_scheduler)),
      is_valid_(true) {
  // Acquire exclusive lock for writing
  frame_->rwlatch_.lock();
  // Mark the page as dirty since we're getting write access
  frame_->is_dirty_ = true;
}
13. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept {}
After
WritePageGuard::WritePageGuard(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept
    : page_id_(that.page_id_),
      frame_(std::move(that.frame_)),
      replacer_(std::move(that.replacer_)),
      bpm_latch_(std::move(that.bpm_latch_)),
      disk_scheduler_(std::move(that.disk_scheduler_)),
      is_valid_(that.is_valid_) {
  // Invalidate the other guard
  that.is_valid_ = false;
}
14. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
auto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & { return *this; }
After
auto WritePageGuard::operator=(WritePageGuard &&that) noexcept -> WritePageGuard & {
  if (this != &that) {
    // Release resources held by this object first
    Drop();

    // Move data from that to this
    page_id_ = that.page_id_;
    frame_ = std::move(that.frame_);
    replacer_ = std::move(that.replacer_);
    bpm_latch_ = std::move(that.bpm_latch_);
    disk_scheduler_ = std::move(that.disk_scheduler_);
    is_valid_ = that.is_valid_;

    // Invalidate the other guard
    that.is_valid_ = false;
  }
  return *this;
}
15. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void WritePageGuard::Flush() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void WritePageGuard::Flush() {
  BUSTUB_ENSURE(is_valid_, "tried to use an invalid write guard");

  if (frame_->is_dirty_) {
    // Create a write request
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();

    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame_->GetDataMut(), page_id_, std::move(promise)});
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);

    // Wait for the write to complete
    future.get();

    // Clear dirty flag
    frame_->is_dirty_ = false;
  }
}
16. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void WritePageGuard::Drop() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void WritePageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Release the exclusive lock first
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();

  // Decrement pin count
  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;

  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable
  if (pin_count == 0) {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Mark this guard as invalid
  is_valid_ = false;
}
17. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::NewPage() -> page_id_t {
  // Atomically increment and return the page id
  return next_page_id_.fetch_add(1);
}
18. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::DeletePage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

  // Check if page is in memory
  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
  if (it == page_table_.end()) {
    // Page not in memory, just deallocate from disk
    disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);
    return true;
  }

  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;
  auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];

  // Check if the page is pinned
  if (frame->pin_count_.load() > 0) {
    return false;
  }

  // Remove from page table
  page_table_.erase(it);

  // Remove from replacer
  replacer_->Remove(frame_id);

  // Reset the frame
  frame->Reset();

  // Add frame back to free list
  free_frames_.push_back(frame_id);

  // Deallocate page from disk
  disk_scheduler_->DeallocatePage(page_id);

  return true;
}
19. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
auto BufferPoolManager::CheckedWritePage(page_id_t page_id, AccessType access_type) -> std::optional<WritePageGuard> {
  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;
  frame_id_t frame_id;

  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

    // Case 1: Check if page is already in the buffer pool
    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
    if (it != page_table_.end()) {
      frame_id = it->second;
      frame = frames_[frame_id];
      // Increment pin count and set as non-evictable
      frame->pin_count_.fetch_add(1);
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_id, false);
      replacer_->RecordAccess(frame_id, page_id, access_type);
      // Return the guard (lock will be acquired in constructor)
      return WritePageGuard(page_id, frame, replacer_, bpm_latch_, disk_scheduler_);
    }

    // Case 2: Page not in buffer pool, need to find a frame
    // First, try to get a free frame
    if (!free_frames_.empty()) {
      frame_id = free_frames_.front();
      free_frames_.pop_front();
    } else {
      // Case 3: No free frames, need to evict
      auto evicted = replacer_->Evict();
      if (!evicted.has_value()) {
        // Could not evict any frame
        return std::nullopt;
      }
      frame_id = evicted.value();

      // Find the page currently in this frame
      for (auto &[pid, fid] : page_table_) {
        if (fid == frame_id) {
          // If dirty, flush to disk
          auto &evicted_frame = frames_[frame_id];
          if (evicted_frame->is_dirty_) {
            auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
            auto future = promise.get_future();
            std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
            requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, evicted_frame->GetDataMut(), pid, std::move(promise)});
            disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
       
... (truncated)

21. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPageUnsafe(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
  if (it == page_table_.end()) {
    return false;
  }

  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;
  auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];

  if (frame->is_dirty_) {
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();
    frame->is_dirty_ = false;
  }

  return true;
}
22. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
auto BufferPoolManager::FlushPage(page_id_t page_id) -> bool {
  std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader> frame;

  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

    auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
    if (it == page_table_.end()) {
      return false;
    }

    frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;
    frame = frames_[frame_id];
  }

  // Take shared lock on the page to flush safely
  std::shared_lock page_lock(frame->rwlatch_);

  if (frame->is_dirty_) {
    auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
    auto future = promise.get_future();
    std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
    requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
    disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
    future.get();
    frame->is_dirty_ = false;
  }

  return true;
}
23. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPagesUnsafe() {
  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

  for (auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {
    auto &frame = frames_[frame_id];
    if (frame->is_dirty_) {
      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
      auto future = promise.get_future();
      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
      requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
      future.get();
      frame->is_dirty_ = false;
    }
  }
}
24. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() { UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation."); }
After
void BufferPoolManager::FlushAllPages() {
  // Get a copy of the page table first
  std::vector<std::pair<page_id_t, std::shared_ptr<FrameHeader>>> pages_to_flush;

  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    for (auto &[page_id, frame_id] : page_table_) {
      pages_to_flush.emplace_back(page_id, frames_[frame_id]);
    }
  }

  // Flush each page safely
  for (auto &[page_id, frame] : pages_to_flush) {
    std::shared_lock page_lock(frame->rwlatch_);
    if (frame->is_dirty_) {
      auto promise = disk_scheduler_->CreatePromise();
      auto future = promise.get_future();
      std::vector<DiskRequest> requests;
      requests.push_back(DiskRequest{true, frame->GetDataMut(), page_id, std::move(promise)});
      disk_scheduler_->Schedule(requests);
      future.get();
      frame->is_dirty_ = false;
    }
  }
}
25. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/buffer/buffer_pool_manager.cpp
Before
auto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {
  UNIMPLEMENTED("TODO(P1): Add implementation.");
}
After
auto BufferPoolManager::GetPinCount(page_id_t page_id) -> std::optional<size_t> {
  std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);

  auto it = page_table_.find(page_id);
  if (it == page_table_.end()) {
    return std::nullopt;
  }

  frame_id_t frame_id = it->second;
  return frames_[frame_id]->pin_count_.load();
}

DiskScheduler::DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager) : disk_manager_(disk_manager) {

  // Spawn the background thread

DiskScheduler::DiskScheduler(DiskManager *disk_manager) : disk_manager_(disk_manager) {
  // Spawn the background thread
  background_thread_.emplace([&] { StartWorkerThread(); });

28. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.cpp
Before
void DiskScheduler::Schedule(std::vector<DiskRequest> &requests) {}
After
void DiskScheduler::Schedule(std::vector<DiskRequest> &requests) {
  for (auto &request : requests) {
    request_queue_.Put(std::move(request));
  }
}
29. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/disk/disk_scheduler.cpp
Before
void DiskScheduler::StartWorkerThread() {}
After
void DiskScheduler::StartWorkerThread() {
  while (true) {
    auto request_opt = request_queue_.Get();

    // Check for shutdown signal
    if (!request_opt.has_value()) {
      return;
    }

    auto &request = request_opt.value();

    if (request.is_write_) {
      // Write page to disk
      disk_manager_->WritePage(request.page_id_, request.data_);
    } else {
      // Read page from disk
      disk_manager_->ReadPage(request.page_id_, request.data_);
    }

    // Signal completion
    request.callback_.set_value(true);
  }
}

  // Determine which list to evict from based on target size
  // If mru_.size() > mru_target_size_, evict from mru
  // Otherwise evict from mfu
  size_t mru_alive_size = mru_.size();

  if (mru_alive_size > mru_target_size_) {
    // Try to evict from mru first
    auto result = evict_from_list(mru_, mru_ghost_);
    if (result.has_value()) {
      return result;

    // If mru has no evictable frames, try mfu
    return evict_from_list(mfu_, mfu_ghost_);

33. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Release the shared lock first
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Decrement pin count
  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;

  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable
  if (pin_count == 0) {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Mark this guard as invalid
  is_valid_ = false;
}
After
void ReadPageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Release the shared lock first
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock_shared();

  // Take BPM latch and decrement pin count atomically with evictable check
  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;
    // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable
    if (pin_count == 0) {
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
    }
  }

  // Mark this guard as invalid
  is_valid_ = false;
}
34. EDIT /workspace/bustub/src/storage/page/page_guard.cpp
Before
void WritePageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Release the exclusive lock first
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();

  // Decrement pin count
  size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;

  // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable
  if (pin_count == 0) {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
  }

  // Mark this guard as invalid
  is_valid_ = false;
}
After
void WritePageGuard::Drop() {
  if (!is_valid_) {
    return;
  }

  // Release the exclusive lock first
  frame_->rwlatch_.unlock();

  // Take BPM latch and decrement pin count atomically with evictable check
  {
    std::scoped_lock lock(*bpm_latch_);
    size_t pin_count = frame_->pin_count_.fetch_sub(1) - 1;
    // If pin count reaches 0, set the frame as evictable
    if (pin_count == 0) {
      replacer_->SetEvictable(frame_->frame_id_, true);
    }
  }

  // Mark this guard as invalid
  is_valid_ = false;
}
